JANSEN, FRANCOIS JOHANNES.—B. 1869; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll.; mag.'s clk., Fraserburg, Feb., 1889; Beaconsfield, Jan., 1890; examr. of accts., C.O., Oct., 1892; civ. comsnr.'s clk. and asst. mag., Barkly West, Dec., 1894; Aliwal North, May, 1895; Dordrecht, July, 1896; ch. clk. and paymr., Cape pol., Kimberley, Nov., 1897; acct., plague admstrn., Apr., 1901; acct., guardians fund, Mar., 1902; sub-comsnr., war losses compensation comsn., Aug., 1902; registr., sup. ct., Eastern dists., Apr., 1904; civ. comsnr., and res. mag., Murraysburg, Oct., 1907; Victoria West, Dec., 1910; ch. clk., dept. of just., Sept., 1917; civ. comsnr., and mag., Uitenhage, Feb., 1923; East London, Feb., 1924.

JEKYLL, CAPTAIN HERBERT, C.M.G. (1885).—Lieutenant royal engineers, 17th April, 1866; employed under War Office and Trinity House in submarine mining and destruction of sunken ships, from 1868 to 1870, and under G.P.O. in the telegraph department, from 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; served in the campaign of 1878-74 on the Gold Coast under Sir Garnet Wolseley; private secretary to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to 3rd Feb., 1878; secretary to the colonial defence commission, 1878; secretary to the royal commission on the defence of British possessions and commerce abroad, September, 1879; private secretary to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, June, 1885, to Jan. 1886.

JELF, ARTHUR SELBORNE, C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhr.); 2nd cls. class. mods., 1897; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1901; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Selangor, Dec., 1903; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1905; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, March, 1905; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Pahang, March, 1907; ag. asst. sec. to res., Perak, Oct., 1908; asst. general adviser to Johore govt. Jan., 1911; offr. of cls. IV., 1st Jan., 1912; ohmn., san. b'd., Kinta, Perak, Apr., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., 16th Sep., 1914; lieut., M.S.V.R., 20th Jan., 1915; seconded for mil. serv. (lieut., general list), Apr., 1917; offr. of cls. III., 12th Dec., 1917; tempy. capt., general staff, S. Sttlmts. command; attchd. general staff, War Office (milit. intell.), Jan., 1918; H.M. Petroleum Executive (sec. to Lord Harcourt's comtee.), June, 1918; offr., cls. II., 1st June, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Perak, Nov., 1919; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1920; dir., Malayan polit. intelligence bureau, cls. 1b, 8th Dec., 1921; mem., Br. Malaya opium comtee., Dec., 1923; ag. contr., lab., S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1924; pres., estates health comsn., Apr., 1924 (recd. thanks of S. of S.); col sec., Jamaica, Oct., 1925; ag. gov., 6th Oct., 1925 to 27th April, 1926; lieut.-col., local forces, Oct., 1927.

JOHNSTON, ALEXANDER R.—Was in the civil service of the Mauritius from the beginning of 1828 until the end of 1833; he then proceeded with the late Lord Napier to China, being appointed his lordship's private secretary; and, in 1835, he became third commissioner, which office he held until appointed deputy superintendent of the trade of British subjects in China in May, 1837; in the latter capacity he was actively employed during the war in China (for which he received the medal), and in superintending the trade in the South during the absence of the chief superintendent in the North; he also administered the government of Hong Kong for upwards of a year, from the first acquisition of that island to its being finally taken over by the colonial department; since 1843, he filled the office of secretary and registrar to the superintendency of trade in China; and on the abolition of that office, Sept. 25, 1852, he obtained a compensation allowance.

flagged_value.position: administrator of the government of Hong Kong

JONES, S. B.—b. 1911; ed. Methodist Boys' High Sch., Freetown, Fourah Bay Coll., and Middle Temple; police mag., S. Leone, 1949; senr., 1958; puisne judge, 1960. (S.L. Govt. service.)

KEITH-ROACH, EDWARD, C.B.E. (1935), O.B.E. (1927), Commdr. of Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1885; ed. privately; 2nd lieut., 7th Lancs. Fuslers., Aug., 1914; capt., Nov., 1914; major, July, 1915; seconded, gen. staff, G.H.Q., E.E.F., July, 1915; Bim, bashi, Egyptian Army, May, 1916; intell. offr., Sudan govt., May, 1916; inspr., Port Sudan-Aug., 1916; Darfur Expedn. and first inspr., Umkedada, Darfur, Oct., 1916 (Sudan Med. and Bar); 2nd cls. mag., 1916; dist. comsnr., Eastern Darfur, Jan., 1917; passed Sudan govt. Arabic exam., 1917; 1st cls. mag., June, 1917; pub. cust., enemy property in Palestine, under occupied enemy territory administrn., Dec., 1919; 1st asst. sec., Palestine govt., 11th Oct., 1920; lent to C.O. by Palestine govt. and ag. prin., Feb., 1924 to Feb., 1925; asst. dist. comsnr., N. dist., 1st Oct., 1925; dep. dist. comsnr., Jerusalem divn., 12th Nov., 1926; publications, "Darfur, a Province of the Anglo-Egyptian Sudan," "The Pageant of Jerusalem," joint editor, "Handbook of Palestine, 1922"; edr., "Gen. Regs. of the Govt. of Palestine."
KENNAN, MYLES.—B. 1894; clk., Basutoland, 13; war serv., 1915-18; sub-inspr., Basutoland counted pol., 1926; clk., dist. comsnr., 1936.

KERSHAW, HENRY VALDER. — Capt., 19th Batt., London Regt., adjutant Nairobi defence force; served as lieut. in 2nd Middlesex Regt. in S. African War, 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps); asst. traffic supt., Jamaica govt. rlwy., Apr., 1906; travelling auditor, Lagos rlwy., Apr., 1908; ag. chief acctnt., Sierra Leone govt. rlwy., Mar., 1909; asst. acctnt., Lagos rlwy., June, 1909 to Dec., 1910; asst. chief acctnt., Uganda rlwy., 23rd Oct., 1912 to 31st Mar., 1915; deputy chief acctnt., Uganda rlwy., 1st Apr., 1915.

KING, CHARLES KEASLEY BERRY.—Ed. Stony-hurst Coll.; matric. Lond. Univ., 1855; 1st clk. and 3rd sub-imigrn. agt., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1871; 2nd sub-imigrn. agt., June, 1872; sen. ditto, Apr., 1881; acted as imigrn. agt.-gen., 1881-2, 1888-9, 1892-3-4-7; mem. of ct. of policy on last three occasions; also mem. poor law bd., and ag. chmn., 1889 and 1894; res. 1896. In 1895, one of the comsns. to report on the best scheme for commutation by E. Indian immigrants of the right of return passages to India; and in 1896, senior mem. of special comtee. to deal with allotment of ld. to I. I. immigrants in lieu of return passage.

KING, JOSEPH ARTHUR. — Copyist. registrar's office Br. Guiana, Jan., 1878; ag. 3rd asst. sworn clk., Mar., 1882; 4th asst. sworn clk., Oct., 1882; 2nd ditto, July, 1883; passed exam. for certif. as sworn clk. and notary public, June, 1885; sworn clk. and notary public, Oct., 1892; admitted solr. of sup. ct., B. Guiana, July, 1894; ag. stip. mag., N.W. dist., May, 1897 to Feb., 1898; J.P. and comsnr. of affidavits; crown solr., Mar., 1898; acted as official receiver for short period in 1906.

KING, NAPLETON WALTER. —B. 1876; cler. asst., surg.-gen's office, Br. Guiana, Apr., 1893; 6th cls. clk., off. of inspr. of prisons, 1896; 5th ditto, June, 1897; 4th cls. offr. of cust., Jan., 1906; asst. supt., alms house, June, 1912; asst. supt., prisons and asst. to inspr., Sept., 1916; ag. supt., prisons and mag., Bartica, Oct.-Dec., 1917; ag. supt., Onderneeming indus. schl. and immigrn. agt., No. 4 dist., Dec., 1918-Jan, 1919 and from Nov., 1920-Feb., 1921; dep. to D.P.W., June, 1919; ag. man. dir., col. steamer serv., Mar.-Dec., 1921; traffic supt., col. transport dept., Jan., 1922; supt., prisons, inspr. of ditto and mag., Bartica, June, 1922.

LEWIS, ARTHUR KENNEDY. —Ed. at Wesleyan high schl. and C.M.S. grammar schl., S. Leone; med. dresser attached to Anglo-French boundary comsn., Dec., 1891, to Apl., 1892 (recd. thanks of govt.); clk. to D.C.; clk. of ct. of requests; registr. of births and deaths, W. dist., 1893 to 1895; 3rd clk. registr.-gen.'s dept., 1895 to 1897; 2nd clk., atty.-gen.'s off., 1898; travelled with and clk. to atty.-gen. during trial of insurgents by Judge G. A. Bonner, 1898; transport clk., N.A. dept., 1899; ditto P. W. D., Jan., 1900, to July, 1901; offr. in charge of transport on govr.'s tours, 1899-1901; regisn. offr., G.P.O., Freetown, Aug., 1901, to Jan., 1903; asst. postmr., Gambia, Feb., 1903.

A. Bonner, 1898; transport clk., N.A. dept., 1899; ditto P. W. LEWIS, JOHN WILLIAMSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Crystal Palace schl. of practical engnrg; jr. asst. engnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rlys., 1909; asst. engnr., 1912; grade III, 1913; senr. dist. engnr., Nov., 1923; ditto, Bahru, May, 1930; do., head office, Mar., 1931; maintenance and new wks. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., Aug., 1932; ag. ch. engnr., ways and wks., Oct., 1934.

LEWIS, JOHN WILLIAMSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Crystal Palace schl. of practical engnrg; jr. asst. engnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rlys., 1909; asst. engnr., 1912; grade III, 1913; senr. dist. engnr., Nov., 1923; ditto, Bahru, May, 1930; do., head office, Mar., 1931; maintenance and new wks. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., Aug., 1932; ag. ch. engnr., ways and wks., Oct., 1934.

LIGHTBOURN, James Le Cato, I.S.O. (1948).—b. 1899 ; ed. Queen's Coll., Nassau, Bah. ; apothecary, govt. hosp., 1904 ; cadet, Out Island admin., 1910 ; sec., bd. of educ., 1914 ; cl. staff, gr. II, 1926 ; gr. I, 1927 ; audr., pub. acctns., Bah., 1939 ; sec., police comsnr., 1921–22.

IGHT, JOSEPH.—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. , Nov., 1921; attd., Galle kach., Jan., 1922; o, Kegalla kach., Feb., 1923; office asst. to govt. agt., Kegalla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., talam, Apr., 1925; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1927; o, Jaffna, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., ticaloa, Jan., 1929; asst. sttlint. offr., Jan., 9; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1933; do., lutara, Apr., 1934; addnl. asst. govt. agt., tara, Nov., 1935.

LIGHTBOURN, J. L.—B. 1889; cadet, Out Islands, Bahamas, 1910; ag. 3rd and 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, 1910-12; sec., bd. of educn., Mar., 1914; sec., pol. comsn., 1921-22; ag. audr., pub. accts., May-Nov., 1924 and from Jan. to Apr., 1926; ag. comsnr., Out Islands, Nov., 1926 to May, 1927; clk., grade I., audit dept., July, 1927; ag. audr., in 1929, 1930 and 1931.

LIGHTBOURN, J. L.—B. 1889; cadet, Out Islands, Bahamas, 1910; ag. 3rd and 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, 1910-12; sec., bd. of educn., Mar., 1914; sec., pol. comsn., 1921-22; ag. audr., pub. accts., May-Nov., 1924 and from Jan. to Apr., 1926; ag. comsnr., Out Islands, Nov., 1926 to May, 1927; clk., grade I., audit dept., July, 1927; ag. audr., in 1929, 1930 and 1931.
LUCAS, C. A. F.—b. 1927; ed. Barrhead H. Sch., Camphill Sec. Sch. and Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; exec. engnr., Tang., 1952; senr., 1962; asst. dir., W.D. & I.D., 1962–64. (Tanzania Govt. service.)

McARTHY, Dennis Douglas, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), M.D. (dist.), N.Z.—b. 1899; ed. Otago High Sch., Otago Univ., Otago Med. Sch., and L.S.H.T.M. (cert.); on mil. serv. 1939-46, col.; med. offr., Zanz., 1927; S.M.O., 1946; Uga., 1946; asst. D.M.S., 1947; malaria res. offr. (C.D.F.), Zanz., 1934-37; mem., E.A. cmd. research comtee., 1941-46; author of Malaria in Zanzibar , 1934-37, and jt. author of Dengue in the Comoro Is. , Dengue Fever in the E.A. Cmd. , 1940-45.

McCARTHY, Dennis Douglas, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), M.D. (dist.), N.Z.—b. 1899 ; ed. Otago High Sch., Otago Univ., Otago Med. Sch., L.S.T.M. & H. cert. ; on mil. serv. 1939-46, col. ; med. offr., Zanz., 1927 ; S.M.O., 1946 ; S.M.O., Uga., 1946 ; A.D.M.S., 1947 ; malaria res. offr. (C.D.F.), Zanz., 1934-37 ; mem., E.A. cmnd. research comtee., 1941-46 ; author of Malaria in Zanzibar , 1934-37 and jt. author of Dengue in the Comoro Is., Dengue Fever in the E.A. Cmd. , 1940-45.

MCGREGOR, ALEXANDER DUNCAN.—B. 1881-2nd grade clk., registr. of mining rights office; 1901; senr. clk., 1910; asst. registr., deeds, Bloem, fontein, O.F.S., 1928; asst. registr., deeds, Pretoria, 1931; registr., deeds, O.F.S., 1934; do., Natal, 1935; registr., mining titles and Rand townships registr., 1937; mem., townships bd., 1937; mem., deeds, rules bd., 1937.

MCELDERRY, SAMUEL BURNSIDE BOYD, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1885; ed. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; math. schl., 1st cl. hons. maths. and experimental science, 1907; B.A., 1907; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., northern dist., New Territories, 1911; ag. dept. registr., sup. ct., 1912; ag. 1st asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., S. Dist., New Territories, 1913; offr. 3rd grade, 1915; temp. in C.O. 1915-1917, and 1919; released for mil. serv., 1918; ag. ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Nov., 1920 to 1921; attd. to secretariat, League of Nations, opium section, 1922; represented Br. Eastern Cols. on opium question at internat. red cross confce., Bangkok, 1922; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1924; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1925; 1st pol. mag., 1925; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1926; rep. Hong Kong at C.O. Confce., May, 1927; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1929; ag. ch. sec., for various periods in 1929, 1930, 1931 and 1933; gov.'s dep. on various occasions; ch. sec., Zanzibar, Dec., 1933; ag. Br. res., various periods, 1934-39.

MCELDERRY, SAMUEL BURNSIDE BOYD, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1885; ed. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; math. schl., 1st cl. hons. maths. and experimental science, 1907; B.A., 1907; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., northern dist., New Territories, 1911; ag. dept. registr., sup. ct., 1912; ag. 1st asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., S. Dist., New Territories, 1913; offr. 3rd grade, 1915; temp. in C.O. 1915-1917, and 1919; released for mil. serv., 1918; ag. ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Nov., 1920 to 1921; attd. to secretariat, League of Nations, opium section, 1922; represented Br. Eastern Cols. on opium question at internat. red cross confce., Bangkok, 1922; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1924; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1925; 1st pol. mag., 1925; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1926; rep. Hong Kong at C.O. Confce., May, 1927; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1929; ag. ch. sec., for various periods in 1929, 1930, 1931 and 1933; gov.'s dep. on various occasions; ch. sec., Zanzibar, Dec., 1933; ag. Br. res., various periods, 1934-39.
MACKENZIE, Alasdair Francis, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.R.C.S., A.I.C.T.A.—b. 1910; ed. Archbishop Tenison's Sch., Royal Coll. of Sci. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; agric. offr., S.L., 1935; snr., 1947.

MACLEAN, George, C.B.E., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—b. 1888. ed. Glasgow Univ., Liverpool Sch. T.M., Alan Milne medal, 1924; on mil. serv. 1918–20 and 1939–42, col.; med. offr., T.T., 1921; sleep. sick offr., 1926; D.D.M.S., 1935; med. advr. and dir. med. serv. Ethiopia (on secondment), 1942–44; D.M.S., Trin., 1945; author of Relationship between economic development and Rhodesian sleeping sickness. History of an outbreak of sleeping sickness in Ufipa

MACLEAN, George, C.B.E., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—b. 1888. ed. Glasgow Univ., Liverpool Sch. T.M., Alan Milne medal, 1924; on mil. serv. 1918–20 and 1939–42, col.; med. offr., T.T., 1921; sleep. sick offr., 1926; D.D.M.S., 1935; med. advr. and dir. med. serv. Ethiopia (on secondment), 1942–44; D.M.S., Trin., 1945; author of Relationship between economic development and Rhodesian sleeping sickness. History of an outbreak of sleeping sickness in Ufipa

MALCOLM, HARCOURT GLADSTONE, O.B.E. (1918); K.C. (1910).—Priv. sec. to Sir Henry when admnstr. of Bahamas, 1892, 1893, 1894 to Sir W. F. Haynes-Smith, gov. has, 1895; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1899; ditto, Bahamas, July, 1899; H. of A., Bahamas, 1900; deputy- 1901; re-elected, 1904 and 1911; ag. comr., Aug. to Nov., 1905; Sept., 1906 to '07; July to Nov., 1910; Apr. to Dec., say to July, and Aug. to Nov., 1913; ag. att., 1913 and 1927; speaker, H. of A., re-elected, 1913, 1919, 1927 and 1928; by S. of S. for assistance to atty.-gen. in getting bills; thanked by legis. for obtaining copies of early records; Bahamas del. Canada-W. Indies confes. at Ottawa, 1920; 1925; Bahamas rep. during visit of Prince les, and parlv. ter-centenary celebrations muda, Oct., 1920; Bahamas del. to W. confce., London, 1926; prepared 1929 edition of the laws of Bahamas. LE. STANLEY FRANCIS.—B. 1897; ed. Wellesley Schl., Somerset and Wadham Coll., Oxford; 2nd mod. language mast., Wellington, Somerset, 1919-20; war serv., France, 1919; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, ; recorder of proceedings, educn. confce., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; sec. 1st regional ml. confnce., E.A. Dependencies, 1929.

MATHEWS, HENRY ADOLPHUS. — Passed through govt. training instn., B. Guiana; 1st cls. certificated schlmstr.; head-master, grant-in-aid schl., B. Guiana, 9½ yrs.; eductnl. dist. offr., 15th Nov., 1893; acted as 2nd asst. inspr. of schools on various occasions, 1895-1903.

MATHEWS, HENRY ADOLPHUS. — Passed through govt. training instn., B. Guiana; 1st cls. certificated schlmstr.; head-master, grant-in-aid schl., B. Guiana, 9½ yrs.; eductnl. dist. offr., 15th Nov., 1893; acted as 2nd asst. inspr. of schools on various occasions, 1895-1903.

MAXWELL, ARTHUR WALTER, M.I.R.S.E.—B. 1885; ed. St. Thomas' Charterhouse, Lon.; ch. tel. mech., N. Nigeria, 1910; asst. signal and tel. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., 1913; asst. tel. engnr., 1917; dist. tel. engnr., 1919; signal and tel. engnr., Feb., 1925.
MAXWELL, JOSEPH P., M.I.C.E. — Pub. wks. dept., N. Zealand, 1874; gen. man., N. Z. rlys. 1880; rly. comsnr. under Rlys. Act, 1887, Jan., 1889.
MIDDLETON, John Reginald.—b. 1918 ; on mil. serv., 1942–46, capt. ; public wks. learner survr., Kingston, J'ca., 1934 ; asst. supt., police, 1948.
MIDDLETON, HON. WM. EDWARD. —B. 1860; ed. Toronto Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; admitted solr., 1884; called to the bar, 1815; K.C., 1908; judge of high ct., Ontario, 1910.
MIDDLETON, HON. WM. EDWARD.—B. 1860; ed. Toronto Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; admitted solr., 1884; called to the bar, 1815; K.C., 1908; judge of high ct., Ontario, 1910.
MILLER, J. B. P.—b. 1903 ; ed. Oxford Univ. ; educ. offr., Ken., 1947 ; senr., 1951 ; provincial educ. offr., 1952.

MILLER, Roger Wallace Rayment.—b. 1908 ; ed. Solihull Sch. and Downing Coll., Camb., B.A., dip. agric. sci. (Cantab.) ; on mil. serv., 1940–46 ; guide demonstrator, I.C.I. agric. res. stn., 1933 ; agriculturist, I.C.I. (India), 1935 ; agric. offr., Mal., 1946 ; senr. agric. offr., Sarawak, 1946 ; dir., agric., 1948.

MILLINGTON, WM. MILNES, B.A. (Oxon).—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Nov., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Jasin, May, 1910; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, 11th Feb., 1911; prom. to cla. V., F.M.S., 3rd Mar., 1911, but continued to act in Dindings; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, 28th Mar., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, surg.-lieut., Antigua def. force, 1904; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla and dep. judge of sup. ct., Leeward Is., Jan., 1905 to Sept., 1906; med. offr., No. 6 dist., Nevis, 1906; med. offr., dist. "D", and mag., dist. "F", Dominica, 1907; med. offr., St. Vincent, 1909-12; temp. capt., R.A.M.C.; med. offr. in charge of troops, Cambridge, 1916-19; founder and hon. sec., W. Indian Club, 1897-8; founder and hon. sec., West India Red Cross Socy., 1921-1924; dir., med. and health dept., Bermuda, Aug., 1924.

MONTAGNAC, LOUIS IVAN DE.—B. 1880; called to bar, Middle Temple, 26th Jan., 1911; clk. of cts., parish of St. Andrew, Jamaica, 1st May, 1921; ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, 1st Aug., 1922; ag. registr., sup. ct., 25th Oct., 1924; registr., sup. ct., 29th July, 1926.

MONTAGNAC, LOUIS IVAN DE.—B. 1880; called to bar, Middle Temple, 26th Jan., 1911; clk. of cts., parish of St. Andrew, Jamaica, 1st May, 1921; ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, 1st Aug., 1922; ag. registr., sup. ct., 25th Oct., 1924; registr., sup. ct., 29th July, 1926.

MOORE, W.—Minister of lands and works, Tasmania, 4th Aug. 1878, to 20th July, 1876, and from 9th to 13th Aug. 1877; colonial secretary, 13th Aug. 1877, to 20th Dec 1878, and again Oct., 1879, to 1881; now president of leg. coun.

MORRIS, ALFRED, A.C.P., M.R.San.Inst.—1874; ed. St. Paul's, Cheltenham; head master, Saiyingpun, Hongkong, 1905-1926; normal school, Queen's Coll., 1914; lecturer, T.I. Teacher 1914-19; English tutor, H. K. Univ., 1915; master of method, H.K. Univ., 1920; ag. supt., St. John Amb. brigade, 1926; head master, King's Coll., 1927; sec., hosp. comforts, 1927; J.P., 1928; ag. dir., tech. inst., 1928; Dec., 1928.

MORRIS, ALFRED, A.C.P., M.R.San.Inst.—1874; ed. St. Paul's, Cheltenham; head master, Saiyingpun, Hongkong, 1905-1926; normal school, Queen's Coll., 1914; lecturer, T.I. Teacher 1914-19; English tutor, H. K. Univ., 1915; master of method, H.K. Univ., 1920; ag. supt., St. John Amb. brigade, 1926; head master, King's Coll., 1927; sec., hosp. comforts, 1927; J.P., 1928; ag. dir., tech. inst., 1928; Dec., 1928.

MORRIS, HON. E. P., K.C.—Mem. Newfoundland assem., 1885-1900; ag. atty.-gen. for Newfnd., 1890-91-95; delegate to Ottawa on confederation question, 1894; delegate to London, 1899, with Rt. Hon. Sir W. V. Whiteway, on matters then before the col. office; leader independent party, Newfnd. assem., 1898-1900; mem. Newfnd. cabinet, 1889-1897-1900; master in chancery, and re-elected for St. John's West, 1900; editor Newfnd. Law Reports Revision, 1820-1900.

MORRISON, C. J.—b. 1919; ed. St. Simon's Coll., J'ca Sch. of Agric. and I.C.T.A.; agric. instr., J'ca, 1943; supvr., agric. instrs., 1947; soil consvn. offr., dept. of agric., 1951; secon., agric. offr., to Yallahs Valley land authy., 1951–53; senr. agric. offr., 1953; offr. i/c experiments and stns.; secon., Christiana land authy., 1954. (J'ca Govt. service.) ☑ MORRISON, D. B.—b. 1918; ed. Broughty Ferry Grove Academy; mil. serv., 1939–46, sgt.; Br. P.O., 1935–49; tech. gr. I, E.A.P. & T. admin., 1949; asst. inspr., traffic, 1952; asst. contrl., telecoms., 1955; senr. asst. contrl., 1959.

MORRISON, C. G. M.—b. 1917; ed. Winchester and Trinity Coll., Camb.; interned, 1941–45; cadet offr., cl. II, H.K., 1939; asst. to supt., imports and exports, 1939; attd. H.K. civ. affrs. London office, 1946; attd. sect. H.Q. civ. affrs. unit, H.K. admin., 1946; asst. P.M.G., 1947; cadet offr., cl. I, 1956; staff gr., 1957; chmn., urban coun. and dir., urban servs., 1958; chmn., housing authy., 1958; redesig. admin. offr., staff gr. A, 1959–63.

MURPHY, M. H.—b. 1911; ed. Ecole St. Jean Baptiste, Papeete, Tahiti; St. Joseph's Coll., H.K., and Lond. Radio Coll.; wireless offr., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1937; A.T.C.O., 1947; telecomm. offr., 1955; sigs. offr., 1961; senr., 1962.

MURPHY, M. H.—b. 1911; ed. Ecole St. Jean Baptiste, Papeete, Tahiti; St. Joseph's Coll., H.K., and Lond. Radio Coll.; wireless offr., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1937; A.T.C.O., 1947; telecomm. offr., 1955; sigs. offr., 1961; senr., 1962.

MURPHY, M. H.—b. 1911; ed. Ecole St. Jean Baptiste, Papeete, Tahiti; St. Joseph's Coll., H.K., and Lond. Radio Coll.; wireless offr., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1937; A.T.C.O., 1947; telecomm. offr., 1955; sigs. offr., 1961; senr., 1962.

MURPHY, M. H.—b. 1911; ed. Ecole St. Jean Baptiste, Papeete, Tahiti; St. Joseph's Coll., H.K., and Lond. Radio Coll.; wireless offr., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1937; A.T.C.O., 1947; telecomm. offr., 1955; sigs. offr., 1961; senr., 1962.

MURPHY, M. H.—b. 1911; ed. Ecole St. Jean Baptiste, Papeete, Tahiti; St. Joseph's Coll., H.K., and Lond. Radio Coll.; wireless offr., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1937; A.T.C.O., 1947; telecomm. offr., 1955; sigs. offr., 1961; senr., 1962.

MURPHY, WILLIAM HENRY WYNDHAM.—B. 1874; ed. Brighton Coll., St. Edmunds, Ware. Army tutors, Versailles; served S. African War, 1899-1902; commanded M. I. Coy., 1st Leinster Regt., Queen's and King's S. A. Medals, 8 clasps, ment. in desps.; transfd., S. A. constab.; asst. dist. comdt. and asst. res. mag., 1903; transfd., Bech. Prot. pol., 1908; rea. J.P.; Reg. Army R. of O.; served European War, France, Flanders and Rhine Army of Occupation; lieut-col., commanded a batt., 1916-19; ag. brigadier, 1916; D.S.O., 1917; ment. in desps.; transfd., Tanganyika Territory as dist. supt. pol., 1920; transfd. Zanzibar, commdt. of pol. and gov. of prisons, 1924; King's pol. med., 1928.

NICOLLS, EDWARD HUGH DYNELEY, C.M.G. (1927), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1871; A.M.I.C.E., 2nd cls. hons. certif. and bracketed first in All England in geom. and perspective drawing, Camb. loc. exams., 1886; supt. of pub. wks., Cyprus, May, 1898; 1st div. engr., Dec., 1898; ag. dir. of pub. wks., Mar., 1903; dir. of pub. wks., Jan., 1904; O/c., A.S.C., Cyprus, in addition to own duties, June, 1915, to Nov., 1916; O/c., R.E., from commencement of the war to date of leaving Cyprus on apptmt. as dir. of pub. wks., Gold Coast, 22nd Nov., 1918; granted local and tempy. rank of capt. from 1st July, 1916, to date of leaving Cyprus; ment. in desps.

NIHILL, JOHN HARRY BARCLAY, K.C., M.A., M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Felsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. hist. tripos, pts. I and II; pres., Union Socy., 1914; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; 2nd lieut., Munster Fusiliers, 1914-18; investigating offr., Min. of Lab., joint industrial couns. divn., 1919; priv. sec. to Sir Wm. Edge, junr. Lord of Treas., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1921; ag. asst. crown solr., 1924; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1926; ag. asst. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; legal sec. to high comsnr., Iraq, 1927-31; ag. Br. cons., Baghdad in addn., 1928 and 1931; mem., Br. deleg. to coun. of L. of N., 1931; legal advr., Embassy, Baghdad, 1932-33; solr.-gen., Uganda, 1934; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1934; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1934 to Feb., 1935; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1936; puisne just., Ceylon, Nov., 1938.

NIHILL, Sir John Harry Barclay, Kt. Bach. (1948), K.C., M.A., M.C.—b. 1892; ed. Felsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge. 2nd cl. hist. tripos, pts. I and II, pres. Union socy., 1914, barrister-at-law, Inner Temple: on mil. serv. 1914–18, 2nd lieut.; cadet, H.K., July, 1921; ag. asst. crown solr., 1924; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1926; ag. asst. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; legal sec. to high comsnr., Iraq. 1927–31; ag. Br. cons., Baghdad, in addn. 1928 and 1931; mem. of Br. delegn. to coun. of L. of N., 1931; legal advsr., Embassy, Baghdad, 1932–33; solr.-gen., Uga., 1934; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1934; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1934–Feb., 1935; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1936; puisne just., Ceylon. Nov., 1938; legal sec., 1942; ch. just., Ken., 1946.

NOOTT, P. GORDON. — B. 1880; entered army (Royal Welsh Fus.), 1898; served in Boer war, 1899-00 (Queen's medal with clasps); Tugela heights, relief of Ladysmith, Cape Colony, Orange Free State and Transvaal; seconded for serv., N. Nigeria regt., Dec., 1904; served with Dakka Kerri expedit., N. Nigeria, 1908; ret. on pension, Oct., 1913; ch. warder, Belize prison, B. Honduras, May, 1914; comsn. 2nd lieut., B. Honduras Terr. Force, 1914; lieut., 1916; served on Cayo expedit., B. Honduras, 1916; instructor to 2nd B. Honduras contingent, 1916; capt., 1918; supt. of prisons, Grenada, Jan., 1920.

NORTHEY, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR EDWARD, G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B.—B. 1868; served with Hazara expedn., 1891; Miranzai expedn., 1891; Isazai expedn., 1892; in S. African war, 1899-02; and in the war of 1914-18; extra A.D.C. to H.M. the King, 1915; brig.-gen. (temp.) commdg. Nyasaland Rhodesian field force, 1915-18; lieut.-col. (brevet col., 1915); King's R.R.C., 1911; gov. E. Africa Prot. and high comsr., Zanzibar Prot., July, 1918; assumed govt., Jan., 1919; ret., Aug., 1922.

NORTHEY, Major-General Sir Edward, G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B.—b. 1868; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; served with Hazara expedn., 1891; Miranzai expedn., 1891; Isazai expedn., 1892, in S.A.; war, 1899–02; and in the war of 1914–18; extra A.D.C. to H.M. the King, 1915; brig.-gen. (temp.) commdg. Nyasa. Rhod. field force, 1915–18; lieut.-col. (brevet col., 1915); King's R.R.C., 1911; gov. E.A.P. and high comsnr., Zanz., July, 1918; assumed govt., Jan., 1919; ret., Aug., 1922; G.O.C. 43rd (The Wessex) Divn. T.A. and S. Western Area, 1924–26.

OLIVIER, S. J.—b. 1905; ed. Sherborne Sch.; mil. serv., 1940-41; O.E.T.A., Ethiopia, 1941-43; sec. to govt., Br. Som. mil. admin., 1943-47; cadet, G.C., 1929; admin. offr., 1932; Nyasa., 1934; dep. prov. comsnr., 1949; secon., fedl. high comsnr's off., London; ret., 1959.
OWEN, A. E.—b. 1920; ed. Colwyn Bay Modern Secondary Sch.; mil. serv., 1940–46; Br. P.O., 1934–49; techn. gr. I, E.A.P. & T. admin., 1949; asst. engr., gr. I, 1955; senr. asst. engr., 1960–62.

PERKINS, HUGH WHARTON, C.B.E. — B. 1886; ed. Berkhamsted Sch.; on active serv., Nov., 1914; D.A.R.T., June, 1915; temp. major, July, 1916; temp. lieut.-col. and asst. dir., rly. transport, Nov., 1917; C.B.E. (mil.), June, 1918; demob., Nov., 1919; ag. traf. man., F.M.S. rlys., Feb., 1925; traf. man., F.M.S. rlys., Oct., 1925.

PERKINS, HUGH WHARTON, C.B.E. — B. 1886; ed. Berkhamsted Sch.; on active serv., Nov., 1914; D.A.R.T., June, 1915; temp. major, July, 1916; temp. lieut.-col. and asst. dir., rly. transport, Nov., 1917; C.B.E. (mil.), June, 1918; demob., Nov., 1919; ag. traf. man., F.M.S. rlys., Feb., 1925; traf. man., F.M.S. rlys., Oct., 1925.

PUDSEY, FAWCETT.—B. 1882; Assoc. M.I.M.E.; Assoc. M.I.C.E.; ed. Hyner's Coll., Hull, and Repton Schl.; apprentice with Messrs. Rose, Downs and Thompson, Ltd., Hull, 1900-1905; engineering asst., dock engnr.'s staff, N.E. Rly., 1905-1911; engaged on survey work, Catalonia, Spain, for the Pearson Engineering Corporation, New York, 1911; in charge of opening of the Hessle quarry and crushing plant of the British Portland Cement Manufacturers, Ltd., 1912-1914; asst. D.P.W., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. D.P.W., Feb., 1916; D.P.W., Palestine, Dec., 1927.

PRINCE, PROF. EDWARD ERNEST, F.R.S.C., &c. — B. 1858; ed. Modern Schl., Leeds, and St. Andrews, Camb., and Edin. Univs.; apptd. demonstr., zool., Edin. Univ., 1885; naturalist, Scotch fishery bd. laboratory, St. Ands.; prof. of zool. and comp. anat., Royal Infirmary Med. Coll., Glasgow, 1890; fishery expert, Irish Deep Sea Survey, 1893; vice-pres. Internat. Fisheries Congress, Washington, Paris, and St. Petersburg, 1903-06-09; vice-pres., biol. sect., Brit. Assoc., 1909; ditto, Royal Soc. of Canada, 1908; mem. of internat. relations coun., American fish. soc., 1910; Canad. mem. of internat. fish. congress, Rome, 1911; life mem. of British Science Guild; mem. of internat. fisheries comsn.; repres. Canada, under Fisheries Treaty, 1908; chmn. of many fishery comsans: lobster, 1898; Pacific salmon, 1907; Bay of Fundy Shad, 1908; Manitoba fisheries, 1909; Alberta and Sask. fisheries, 1910-1911; chmn. of biol. bd. of Canada; comsnr. of fisheries for Canada, apptd. 1892.

RAINSFORD, CAPT. RICHARD FREDERICK. — B. 1888; Natal pol., 1904-07, served Zulu Rebellion, 1906, (med.); B.S.A.P., 1907-08; inspr. E.A. Pol., 1910; asst. supt., 1914; commanded No. 1 Column, Turkhana Punitive Expedn., 1915 (desps., A.G.S. med.); capt., 5th K.A.R., 1916; adjt. and staff offr. to O.C. troops, Jubaland, 1917-19, (desps.); "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory meds.; supdt., Kenya pol., 1923.

REAKES, CHARLES JOHN, M.R.C.V.S., Eng., Doctor of Vet. Science, Univ. of Melbourne. — B. 1865; apptd. vet. to New Zealand govt., 1897; ch. vet., 1908; lieut.-col., N.Z. vet. corps, 1908; colonel, 1915; dir. of vet. services and remounts, N.Z. defence dept., 1908; dir., live stock div., agric. dept., 1909; dir.-gen. of agric., 1918.

REAKES, CHARLES JOHN, M.R.C.V.S., Eng., Doctor of Vet. Science, Univ. of Melbourne. — B. 1865; apptd. vet. to New Zealand govt., 1897; ch. vet., 1908; lieut.-col., N.Z. vet. corps, 1908; colonel, 1915; dir. of vet. services and remounts, N.Z. defence dept., 1908; dir., live stock div., agric. dept., 1909; dir.-gen. of agric., 1918.

REED, HON. SIR JOHN RANKEN, Kt. Bach. (1936), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1864; ed. Gram. Schl., Auckland, N.Z., Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1921-39; pres., prisons bd., 1928; judge advoc.-gen., N.Z. Defence Forces, with rank of colonel, 1911-33.
RICHARDS, HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, LL.D.—Crown solr., S. Australia, 1916; formerly parliamentary draftsman; ag. judge, sup. ct., 1925; judge, ditto, 1927.
RICHARDS, HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, LL.D.—Crown solr., S. Australia, 1916; formerly parliamentary draftsman; ag. judge, sup. ct., 1925; judge, ditto, 1927.
RICHARDSON, R. K.—b. 1918; ed. Pamphylian High Sch. and St. Mary's Coll., Trin., St. Mungo's Med. Sch., Glasgow; M.O., gr. C, Trin., 1946; gr. B, 1950; gr. A (T.B. div.), 1952. (T'dad Govt. service.)
RICHARDSON, R. K.—b. 1918; ed. Pamphylian High Sch. and St. Mary's Coll., Trin., St. Mungo's Med. Sch., Glasgow; M.O., gr. C, Trin., 1946; gr. B, 1950; gr. A (T.B. div.), 1952. (T'dad Govt. service.)

ROBERTS, J. D.—b. 1923; ed. Canton High Sch., Cardiff; mil. serv. 1941–46; Br. P.O. 1937–1952; E.A.P. & T. admin., postmaster gr. II, 1952; sales and serv. supt., 1956; chief telecoms. supt., 1960; tele. sales mangr., 1961–62.

ROBERTS, J. D.—b. 1923; ed. Canton High Sch., Cardiff; mil. serv. 1941–46; Br. P.O. 1937–1952; E.A.P. & T. admin., postmaster gr. II, 1952; sales and serv. supt., 1956; chief telecoms. supt., 1960; tele. sales mangr., 1961–62.

ROBERTS, J. D.—b. 1923; ed. Canton High Sch., Cardiff; mil. serv. 1941–46; Br. P.O. 1937–1952; E.A.P. & T. admin., postmaster gr. II, 1952; sales and serv. supt., 1956; chief telecoms. supt., 1960; tele. sales mangr., 1961–62.

ROBERTS, J. D.—b. 1923; ed. Canton High Sch., Cardiff; mil. serv. 1941–46; Br. P.O. 1937–1952; E.A.P. & T. admin., postmaster gr. II, 1952; sales and serv. supt., 1956; chief telecoms. supt., 1960; tele. sales mangr., 1961–62.
ROBERTS, CAPT. WILLIAM HOWARD.—B. 1887; asst. engnr., F.M.S., June, 1920; exec. engnr., June, 1929; do., P.W., Dec., 1933; do., S'pre, Oct., 1934; ag. snr. exec. engnr., Feb., 1936.

ROBERTS, GEORGE FURSE.—B. 1881; ed. Univ. Coll., London; telegraphist, G.P.O., Sept., 1897; 2nd div. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Apr., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1905; off. asst. to govt. agt., Uva Prov., Apr., 1907; pol. mag., Panadure, Jan., 1909; Jaffna, Dec., 1909; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, and comsnr. of requests and pol. mag., Nuwara Eliya, May, 1912; pol. mag., Galle, Mar., 1915; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1916; tea comsnr., Nov., 1918; contr., estate supplies, in additn. to his own duties, May, 1919; comsnr., requests, Colombo. Nov., 1921; rubber contr., Oct., 1922; dist. judge, Kurunegala. Nov., 1925; ditto, Negombo, Nov., 1926; regisr.-gen. and regisr., motor cars, Nov., 1928; addnl. dist. judge, Kandy, June, 1931; comsnr., requests Kandy in addn., Oct., 1931; dist. judge, Galle, Jan., 1935.

ROCHE, CAPTAIN W. P.—Late 3rd (Prince of Wales') Dragoon Guards educated at the Royal Military College, Sandhurst; served in India, and throughout the Abyssinian campaign (medal). First writer and superintendent of police, Gambia, May, 1879; clerk of the legislative council; acting collector and treasurer, July, 1880, till Jan., 1881, and again, Jan. till May, 1881; government secretary, July, 1883; superintendent of police, Grenada, 1886.

ROCHE, CAPTAIN W. P.—Late 3rd (Prince of Wales') Dragoon Guards; educated at the Royal Military College, Sandhurst; served twice in India, and throughout the Abyssinian campaign under Lord Napier of Magdala (medal). Appointed first writer and superintendent of police, the Gambia, May, 1879; clerk of the legislative council; is a justice of the peace; acting collector and treasurer from 1st July, 1880, till 14th Jan., 1881, and again from 22nd Jan. till 7th May, 1881; government secretary, July, 1883.

ROGERS, W. B., O.B.E. (1945), E.R.D.—b. 1914; ed. Charterhouse; mil. serv., 1939-46, col.; cadet, Tang., 1950; estab. offr., 1950; prin. asst. sec., 1955; admin. offr., cl. IIA, 1957; cl. I, 1959; trade rep. for Tang. in Fed. of Rhod. & Nyasa., 1959; perm. sec. (ext. affairs and def.), 1961-62; devel. comsnr., Fiji, 1963; Fiji gov't's rep., Australia, 1964; hd. of security secretariat, Aden, 1965.

ROTH, WALTER E.—B.A. (Oxon), late Demy of Magd. Coll.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P. (Lond.); hon. mem. anthrop. societies of Berlin and Florence, and of anthrop. inst., Lond.; late ch. protector of aborigines, Queensland; and Royal comsnr. to inquire into condition of natives of W. Aust.; stip. mag., dep. prot. of Indians, and govt. med. offr., Pomeroon River, B. Guiana. 8th Dec., 1906; stip. mag., Demerara River, 1915; author of "Elements of School Hygiene," 1886, "Theatre Hygiene," 1888, "Ethnological Studies among the N.W. Central Aborigines, Queensland," 1897, "N. Queensland Ethnography Bulletins," "Superstitions, Magic and Medicine," 1903; "Burial Ceremonies and Disposal of the Dead," 1907, "Animism and Folk-lore of the Guiana Indians," 1915, "Arts, Crafts and Customs of the Guiana Indians," 1923, translator of Richard Schomburgk's "Travels in Br. Guiana," 1923.

H.K., 1946; mem., advsy. comtee. on educ. in the colonies, 1942; sec., W.I. higher educ. comtee., 1944; mem., comtee. on mass educ., etc.

SALIER, LIEUT.-COL. EDWARD LUCAS.—B. 1882; ed. Hutchins Schl., Hobart, Tasmania; 2nd lieut., 4th K.S.L.I., 1903; Northumberland Fusiliers, 1906; India, N.W. Frontier, 1908; seconded S. Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F., 1910; asst. regtl. adjt., S.N. Regt., 1910; regtl. adj., S.N. Regt., 1911-13; adjt., 3rd S.N. Regt., 1914; S.O., Fly and Cross River Columns, W.A.F.F., Cameroons, 1914; adjt., 2nd Northumberland Fusiliers, France, Belgium, Salonika, 1915; severely wounded, 1915; brig. major, 12th Australian Infy. Brig., France, Belgium, 1916-17; brevet major, June, 1917; G.S.O. II, Tank Corps, 1917; in commd., 1st Northumberland Fusiliers, 1918; G.S.O. I, Tank Corps, 1918; temp. lieut.-col., 1918; six times ment. in desps.; M.C., Legion of Honour, brevet major; in commd., 4th Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F., 1920-21; Reg. Army, R. of O., Lieut.-Col., May, 1921; resigned, 31st Mar., 1925; contr., lab., Ascension Island (St. Helena Colony), Aug., 1925; pol. mag., offr. in ch., secretariat, ag. treas. and collr., cust. and clerk to exec. coun., St. Helena, May, 1928; govt. sec., Jan., 1929.

SALIER, LIEUT.-COL. EDWARD LUCAS.—B. 1882; ed. Hutchins Schl., Hobart, Tasmania; 2nd lieut., 4th K.S.L.I., 1903; Northumberland Fusiliers, 1906; India, N.W. Frontier, 1908; seconded S. Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F., 1910; asst. regtl. adjt., S.N. Regt., 1910; regtl. adj., S.N. Regt., 1911-13; adjt., 3rd S.N. Regt., 1914; S.O., Fly and Cross River Columns, W.A.F.F., Cameroons, 1914; adjt., 2nd Northumberland Fusiliers, France, Belgium, Salonika, 1915; severely wounded, 1915; brig. major, 12th Australian Infy. Brig., France, Belgium, 1916-17; brevet major, June, 1917; G.S.O. II, Tank Corps, 1917; in commd., 1st Northumberland Fusiliers, 1918; G.S.O. I, Tank Corps, 1918; temp. lieut.-col., 1918; six times ment. in desps.; M.C., Legion of Honour, brevet major; in commd., 4th Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F., 1920-21; Reg. Army, R. of O., Lieut.-Col., May, 1921; resigned, 31st Mar., 1925; contr., lab., Ascension Island (St. Helena Colony), Aug., 1925; pol. mag., offr. in ch., secretariat, ag. treas. and collr., cust. and clerk to exec. coun., St. Helena, May, 1928; govt. sec., Jan., 1929.

SANSOM, CHARLES HENRY, C.M.G. (1938)—B. 1886; ed. Plymouth and Munnamead Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1923; pol. prob., 1905; regisar. of criminals, F.M.S. and S.S., 1910; asst. comsnr. of pol., Ipoh, 1911; adjt., F.M.S. pol., 1913; comsnr. of pol., Johore, 1921; supt., pol., S'pore but cont. former appt., Nov., 1924; ch. pol. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1929; offg. ch. pol. offr., Perak, Nov., 1930; comsnr., pol., F.M.S., Oct., 1931.

SCOTT, Sir Henry Harold, K.C.M.G. (1941), C.M.G. (1936), M.D., F.R.C.P. (Lond.) ; D.P.H. ; D.T.M. & H. (Camb.) ; F.R.S.E., F.Z.S.—b. 1874 ; ed. Mercers, City of Lond., Univ. Coll., St. Barts. and St. Thos. Hosp., and Copenhagen ; res. M.O., Teignmouth, Dawlish and Newton Abbot Inf. ; hse. physician, St. Thos. ; R.A.M.C. capt., S.A. war ; European war ; pathol., Cambridge Hosp., Aldershot, i/c mobile laboratory No. 18 ; Milner Res. Fellow in comp. path., L.S.H. and T.M. ; pathol., Zoological Soc. of Lond. ; gov. bacteriologist, J'ca., 1910 ; H.K., 1919 ; lect., trop. dis., Westminster Hosp. med sch. ; dir., bureau of hyg. and trop. diseases, 1935–42 ; examr. in trop. med. and surg., conjt. bd. of R.C.P. and R.C.S. ; med. sec., col. med. res. comtee. ; examr. for dip. in T.M. and T.H. and in parasit. for D.P.H. and the B. Vet. Sc., Liverpool Univ. ; FitzPatrick lect., R.C.P. ; author of A History of Tropical Medicine, Some Notable Epidemics, Post-graduate Clinical Studies, Health Problems of the Empire (with Sir Andrew Balfour), Tuberculosis in Man and Lower Animals and British Red Cross Manual of Tropical Hygiene (with Maj.-Gen. D. T. Richardson), 1946.

SCRIVENOR, JOHN BROOKE, I.S.O. (1931).—B. 1876; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; served on geol. surv. of the U.K. in 1902-03; geologist, F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. senr. warden, mines, F.M.S., in addn., Mar.-Sept., 1908; served in the Army in England and France from Dec., 1916 to end of war as 2nd lieut., R.E. signal service; on sp. duty for state of Kelantan, May, 1924; dir., geological survey, F.M.S., Dec. 1927.

SEMPER, D. H., I.S.O. (1930).—Clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner for dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. registr., Leeward Is., and registr. and prov.-marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., registr. of titles, registr. of friendly societies, and registr.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; apptd. registr. and prov.-marshal, admnstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., and registr. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905; ag. mag., dist. C., Feb., 1911; M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1911; escheator-gen., 1911; offr. in charge of prison discipline, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1911; addtl. mag., Dist. C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1916; mag. and coroner, dist. D., 30th Nov., 1921.

SEMPER, D. H., I.S.O. (1930).—Clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner for dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. registr., Leeward Is., and registr. and prov.-marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., registr. of titles, registr. of friendly societies, and registr.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; apptd. registr. and prov.-marshal, admnstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., and registr. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905; ag. mag., dist. C., Feb., 1911; M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1911; escheator-gen., 1911; offr. in charge of prison discipline, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1911; addtl. mag., Dist. C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1916; mag. and coroner, dist. D., 30th Nov., 1921.

SHAW, GEORGE ERNEST, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; scholar and senr. moderator in classics; vice-chancellor's prizeman in English prose; B.A., LL.B.; cadet, F.M.S. civ. ser., 1900; collr., land rev., Krian, 1901; mag., 1st cls., Perak, 1903; offr., Cls. V., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1906; collr., land rev., Lower Perak, 1908; adviser, land office, Kedah, and supt. of mines, 1911; offr., Cls. IV., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; supermny. offr., Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; lent to W.O., directorate of prisoners of war, Aug., 1916; adviser, land off., Kedah, 19th July, 1919; ag. comsnr., lands and mines, Johore, May, 1923; ag. sec., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1924; on sp. duty, Burma, Oct., 1925; ag. sec., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1925; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, Dec., 1925; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Sept., 1927; gen. advr., Johore, Feb., 1929.

SHEPPARD, WALTER SYDNEY, B.A., M.B., B. Ch. (Cantab). — B. 1871; civil surg. in charge of H.M.'s troops, N.E. dist., Eng., May, 1898, to May, 1899; spec. plague med. offr., India, May, 1899, to Nov., 1900; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 19th Nov., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Sttlnts., 1st Mar., 1901; spec. mission to Brunei, Aug., 1904; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1905; senior med. offr., Singapore, July, 1911.

SHELLEY, MALCOLM BOND.—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1901; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1902; ag. asst. supt., immigrs., 1906; ag. 2nd asst. sec. to res.-gen., 1907; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1910; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, 1911; comsn. in M.S. Vol. Rifles, 1911; ag. asst. registr., sup. ct., Ipoh, 1912; ag. dist. offr., Larut, 1914; ag. inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., 1914; capt., M.S.V.R., 1915; ag. dep. pub. pros., Perak, 1916; ag. mag., Ipoh, 1920; off. assignee and pub. trustee, 1923; ag. dep. treas., Singapore, 1924; ag. treas., S.S., 1924; ag. dep. treas., Singapore, 1924; ag. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1925; chmn., trade statistics comtee., Aug., 1926; contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1926; treas., S.S., 1929; ag. dir. educn. S.S. and F.M.S. and pres. Raffles Coll., Dec., 1929; ag. col. sec., S.S., Oct., 1930.

SIDLEY, William Arthur deBurgh.—b. 1911; ed. Clifton Coll. and Univ. of Bristol; police constab., Pal., 1934; cpl., 1936; sgt., 1937; inspr., B. Hond., 1938; asst. supt., 1940; Nig. 1946; senr. asst., 1947; seconded B. Hond. as ag. dist. comsnr., 1943; twice visited Mexico for liaison with authorities in conn. with an extradition, 1944.

SIDLEY, William Arthur deBurgh.—b. 1911; ed. Clifton Coll. and Univ. of Bristol; police constab., Pal., 1934; cpl., 1936; sgt., 1937; inspr., B. Hond., 1938; asst. supt., 1940; Nig. 1946; senr. asst., 1947; seconded B. Hond. as ag. dist. comsnr., 1943; twice visited Mexico for liaison with authorities in conn. with an extradition, 1944.

SIDLEY, William Arthur deBurgh.—b. 1911; ed. Clifton Coll. and Univ. of Bristol; police constab., Pal., 1934; cpl., 1936; sgt., 1937; inspr., B. Hond., 1938; asst. supt., 1940; Nig. 1946; senr. asst., 1947; seconded B. Hond. as ag. dist. comsnr., 1943; twice visited Mexico for liaison with authorities in conn. with an extradition, 1944.

SIMPSON, SAMUEL, C.M.G. (1926).—B. 1876; ed. at Owen's Coll., and Edin. Univ.; B.Sc.: Stevens scholar; sec. to the Union; sen. pres. of students' representative coun.; Highland and agric. socy.'s prizeman; life mem. and silver medallist of roy. agric. socy.; holder of nat. diploma in agric.; sen. lecturer in agric. to govt. agric. coll., Ghizeh, Egypt; cotton expert to B.O.A. Prot., Mar., 1906; dir. of agric., Uganda, 1912; mem., leg. and exec. couns.; comsnr. for Uganda at Br. Emp. Exhibn.; author of "Report on Cotton Growing Industry in B.C.A. Prot."; reported on the agric. resources of Angola, Trinidad, Tobago, B. Guiana and Surinam.

SHELSWELL-WHITE, GEOFFREY HENRY, Barrister-at-Law. —B. 1897; ed. Whitgift Schl.; comsnd. with R.F.A. in India, Mesopotamia and Persia, 1916-18; asst. pol. offr., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; personal asst. to Sir Arnold Wilson, ag. civ. comsnr., 1918; Iraq rebellion, 1920; A.D.C., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to res., ag. 1st asst. sec., secretariat, clk. to prot. coun. and edr., off. gaz. at various periods; higher standard Swahili, 1925; pres., Pemba Swahili exam. bd., 1929; dist. registr., Pemba, 1929 and 1930; ag. res. mag., Pemba, 1929 and 1930; called to bar, 1930; pvte. sec. to Sultan, 1930-35; asst. sec., secretariat, 1930-35; clk., exec. and leg. couns., 1931-35; edr., Offl. Gaz., 1931-33; trades licensing comsnr., 1934-35; ag. asst. ch. sec., 1934; ag. asst. ch. sec. and prov. comsnr., 1935; Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1936; ch. asst. sec., Gibraltar, 1936; ag. col. sec., May-Sept., 1937; fin. advers. Trans-Jordan, 1938; author of "A Guide to Zanzibar."

SINCLAIR, J. C.—b. 1912; ed. Cornwall Coll., J'ca; asst., treasury, J'ca, 1933; 2nd cl. clk., 1939; 1st cl., 1943; senr. clk., 1947; senr. acctnt., police, 1953; currency offr., 1957; prin. asst. sec., min. of housing and soc. welfare, 1959. (J'ca Govt. service.)

SINCLAIR, J. C.—b. 1912; ed. Cornwall Coll., J'ca; asst., treasury, J'ca, 1933; 2nd cl. clk., 1939; 1st cl., 1943; senr. clk., 1947; senr. acctnt., police, 1953; currency offr., 1957; prin. asst. sec., min. of housing and soc. welfare, 1959. (J'ca Govt. service.)

SINCLAIR, J. C.—b. 1912; ed. Cornwall Coll., J'ca; asst., treasury, J'ca, 1933; 2nd cl. clk., 1939; 1st cl., 1943; senr. clk., 1947; senr. acctnt., police, 1953; currency offr., 1957; prin. asst. sec., min. of housing and soc. welfare, 1959. (J'ca Govt. service.)

SINCLAIR, J. C.—b. 1912; ed. Cornwall Coll., J'ca; asst., treasury, J'ca, 1933; 2nd cl. clk., 1939; 1st cl., 1943; senr. clk., 1947; senr. acctnt., police, 1953; currency offr., 1957; prin. asst. sec., min. of housing and soc. welfare, 1959. (J'ca Govt. service.)

SINCLAIR, J. C.—b. 1912; ed. Cornwall Coll., J'ca; asst., treasury, J'ca, 1933; 2nd cl. clk., 1939; 1st cl., 1943; senr. clk., 1947; senr. acctnt., police, 1953; currency offr., 1957; prin. asst. sec., min. of housing and soc. welfare, 1959. (J'ca Govt. service.)

SINCLAIR, J. C.—b. 1912; ed. Cornwall Coll., J'ca; asst., treasury, J'ca, 1933; 2nd cl. clk., 1939; 1st cl., 1943; senr. clk., 1947; senr. acctnt., police, 1953; currency offr., 1957; prin. asst. sec., min. of housing and soc. welfare, 1959. (J'ca Govt. service.)

SINCLAIR, WM. — Clk., stores dept., L and N.W. rly., Jan., 1863; stores auditor, July, 1875; rly. storekeeper, Midland system, Cape govt. rlys., Aug., 1878; ch. rly. storekeeper, Jan., 1886; storekeeper, C.S.A.R., 1904.

SINCLAIR, WM. — Clk., stores dept., L and N.W. rly., Jan., 1863; stores auditor, July, 1875; rly. storekeeper, Midland system, Cape govt. rlys., Aug., 1878; ch. rly. storekeeper, Jan., 1886; storekeeper, C.S.A.R., 1904.

SINCLAIR, WM. — B. 1844; clk., stores dept., L. and N.W. rly., Jan., 1863; stores auditor, July, 1875; rly. storekeeper, Midland system, Cape govt. rlys., Aug., 1878; ch. rly. storekeeper, Jan., 1886; mem. of tender bd., July, 1901; ch. rly. storekeeper, C.S.A.R., Dec., 1903; supt. of stores and shipping branch, Transvaal agt.-gen.'s office, London, July, 1907.

SINCLAIR, WM. — B. 1844; clk., stores dept., L. and N.W. rly., Jan., 1863; stores auditor, July, 1875; rly. storekeeper, Midland system, Cape govt. rlys., Aug., 1878; ch. rly. storekeeper, Jan., 1886; mem. of tender bd., July, 1901; ch. rly. storekeeper, C.S.A.R., Dec., 1903; supt. of stores and shipping branch, Transvaal agt.-gen.'s office, London, July, 1907.

SINCLAIR, JOHN HOUSTON, C.M.G. (1915), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1871; apptd. to col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., Dec., 1893; local auditor, E.A.P. and Uganda Rlwy., Dec., 1895; H.M. vice-consul, Zanzibar, Apl., 1899; ag. agt. and consul-gen., Zanzibar, Nov., 1903, to June, 1904, Feb. to May, 1905; consul, 1st July, 1906; ag. agt. and consul-gen., Aug., 1906, to Feb., 1907, Mar. to Oct., 1911, and Feb., 1913 to Apl., 1914; ch., sec. Zanzibar Prot., 1st Jan., 1914; pres. of internat. bureau at Zanzibar for the suppression of the slave trade, 1914 and 1916; ag. res., Aug., 1916, to Apl., 1917, and from Mar. to Dec., 1919; also ag. high comsnr., 8th Nov. to 2nd Dec., 1919; ag. high comsnr. and res., 8th Aug. to 1st Oct., 1920; ag. res., 1st Oct., 1921; res., 10th Jan., 1922.

SKIRVING, JOHN MACKENZIE.—B. 1874; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; served nine years in 91st Highrs.; 1st cls. cert. of educn., Group I, 1894; cert. of mil. eng., 1896; on Boer prisoners of war staff as warrant offr., S. Africa and St. Helena, Nov., 1899, to Oct., 1902 (Queen's medal and clasp); inspr. of pol., gaoler and mag.'s clk., St. Helena, Mar., 1903; served in various offl. capacities in St. Helena, Antigua, Montserrat and Dominica, 1906-14; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Islands; ag. mag., Dist. F., May-Aug., 1914; capt. (2nd in command), Dominica defence force, 4th Aug., 1914; comdg. local forces, Dominica, 13th Sept., 1914; in command of 3rd (Leeward Is.) contingent for B.W.I.R., at Barbados, June-July, 1917; also paying offr. and offr.-in-charge records and dispersal, B.W.I. Regt., Dominica, from 1st Nov., 1917 to 29th Oct., 1920; inspr. pol., offr. instr. to local forces and in charge of prison, fire brig. and weights and measures depts., Montserrat, Oct., 1920; ag. in charge of P.W.D., and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1923; re-transfd. to Antigua as ch. keeper of prisons, supt., St. John's Training Schl. and supt., pauper cemetery, Leeward Is., 1st Nov., 1924; dep. coroner, 1926.

SKIRVING, JOHN MACKENZIE.—B. 1874; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; served nine years in 91st Highrs.; 1st cls. certif. of eductn., Group I, 1894; certif. of mil. eng., 1896; on Boer prisoners of war staff as warrant offr., S. Africa and St. Helena, Nov., 1899, to Oct., 1902 (Queen's medal and clasp); inspr. of pol., gaoler and mag.'s clk., St. Helena, Mar., 1903; sergt.-major and drill inst., St. Helena volrs.; also sanitary inspr., firemaster and inspr. of weights and measures, 1906; also schl. attendance offr., offr. in charge of Zulu prison, and asst. chief clk. in govr.'s off., 1907-1909; transfd. to Leeward Islands pol., and apptd. to Antigua in June, 1909, where sub.-inspr. of pol., and mil. instr. to defence force and defence reserve; transfd. to Montserrat in July, 1910, where sub-inspr. of pol., inspr. of weights and measures, inspr. of works and roads, mil. instr. to defence reserve, mem. of Board of Health, and visiting justice of prison; then offr. in charge of prison discipline; transfd. to Dominica in May, 1912, where inspr. of pol., mil. instr. to defence reserve, offr. in charge of prison discipline, inspr. of weights and measures, supt. of Roseau fire brigade, offr. in charge of powder magazine, and mem. of Roseau town bd. and bd. of cemetery comsns.; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Islands; ag. mag., Dist. F., May-Aug., 1914; capt. (2nd in command), Dominica defence force, 4th Aug., 1914; comdg. local forces, Dominica, 13th Sept., 1914; in command of 3rd (Leeward Is.) contingent for B.W.I.R., at Barbados, June-July, 1917; also paying offr. and offr.-in-charge records and dispersal, B.W.I. Regt., Dominica, from 1st Nov., 1917; inspr. pol., offr. instr. to local forces and in charge of prison, fire brig. and weights and measures depts., Montserrat, Oct., 1920; ag. in charge of P.W.D., and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1923.

SMART, LESLIE MASON, C.B.E. (1936)—B. 1889; asst. supt., Kenya and Uganda Rly., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1927; dep. traffic man., Tanganyika rlys., Mar., 1927; gen. man., Gold Coast rly. and hbr. authority, Takoradi hbr., Oct., 1933; gen. man., F.M.S. rlys., 1937.

SLOLEY, SIR HERBERT CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1905).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. comsnr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. comsnr., July to Dec., 1895; govt. sec., 1898; ag. res. comsnr., Dec., 1900; res. comsnr., Sept., 1901; ret., 1926.

SMIT, HAROLD PRINGLE.—B. 1883; joined Cape civ. serv., Jan., 1901; office of dir. of mil. intell., May-Aug., 1902; Transvaal law dept., Sept., 1902; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., Transvaal, July, 1908; addl. mag., Benoni, Sept., 1914; war stores comsn., 1915; mag., Bloemhof dist., Dec., 1915; ch. clk., dept. of just., Dec., 1917; pub. serv. inspr., Feb., 1922; sec. for S.W. Africa, Apr., 1923; dep. admstr., May-Oct., 1924 and July-Dec., 1928; sec. for lands, Feb., 1934; contr. and audr.-gen., Nov., 1935.

SMALLEY, JAMES THORNTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1882; late clinical asst., asst. house surg., out-pat. offr. and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical asst., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji; D.M.O., Ra; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-1913; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong, med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly., and med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, 1913; lecturer in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examr., physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital; med. offr. in charge, Lai Chi Kok branch prison; surg. lieut., Hong Kong Volunteer Corps; med. offr. govt. civil hosp., 1923; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Nov., 1927 to Jan., 1928; ag. dep. dir., med. and sany. services, Feb., 1928 to Feb., 1929; snr. med. offr. and in ch., Kowloon hosp., Jan., 1931; ag. D.D.M.S., June, 1937 and in addn. ag. D.M.S., July, 1937; ag. D.D.M.S., Mar., 1938; lecturer in pub. health, H.K. Univ.

SMITH, C. A. JEFFREY.—Apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Jamaica, Aug., 1870; asst. to ch. supt., 1875; 3rd class ditto, 1879; 2nd class ditto, 1881; ag. dist. engintr., W. dist., 1886; ditto, Kingston dist., 1888-9; overseer of wks., prisons dept., 1889; 1st cls. supt., pub. wks., and supt., St. Catherine waterwks., 1896; also acted on several occasions as govt. assessor, and supt. irrigation wks.

SMITH, Clarence Edward, L.M.S. (S'pore).—b. 1904; ed. King Edward VII Coll. of Med., S'pore.; asst. surg., S'pore., 1927; asst. med. offr., 1932; gr. I, 1935; med. offr., Mal. med. serv., 1946; tutor in med., 1947.

SMITH, FREDERICK STANLEY.—Ed. at Sherborne Coll., Eng.; called to the bar, In. Temp., Jan., 1892; ag. pol. mag., dist. "B.," Barbados, Aug. to Oct., 1893; ag. pol. mag., dist. "D.," Apr., 1894, to July, 1895; and from Dec., 1895, to Oct., 1896; ag. pol. mag., dist. "C.," Jan. to Feb., 1897; J.P., Apr., 1897; ag. judge-asst., ct. of appeal, Apr. to Sept., 1897; ag. pol. mag., dist. "E.," Jan., 1898; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown and dist. "A.," Feb. to May, 1898; ag. pol. mag., dist. "F.," Sept. to Oct., 1898; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "D.," Jan., 1899; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown and dist. "A.," Nov., 1901, to Apr., 1902; mag., dist. "A." 1902; ag. judge asst. ct. of appeal on several occasions.

SMITH, FREDERICK STANLEY.—Ed. at Sherborne Coll., Eng.; called to the bar, In. Temp., Jan., 1892; ag. pol. mag., dist. "B.," Barbados, Aug. to Oct., 1893; ag. pol. mag., dist. "D.," Apr., 1894, to July, 1895; and from Dec., 1895, to Oct., 1896; ag. pol. mag., dist. SMITH, HON. WILLIAM HENRY LAIRD.—Represents Denison, Tasmania, in H. of R., C. of A.; asst. min, C. of A., 14th Nov., 1916.

SPOONER, MAJOR JOHN CHARLES GARTH, M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Marlborough Coll.; jr. asst. engr., F.M.S. rlys., 1903-06; asst. engr., F.M.S. rlys., 1906-12; dist. engr., grade III, 1912-14; dist. engr., grade II, 1914; on active serv., 1915-19; dist. engr., grade I, Ipoh, Jan., 1920; sen. dist. engr., Ipoh, Jan., 1921; senr. dist. engr., J. Bahru, Mar., 1927; ditto, K. Lumpur, Nov., 1928; engr., ways and wks., June, 1929. S POWERS, JAMES HOWARD.—B. 1889; authorised survr., Queensland, Oct., 1911; survr., F.M.S., Nov., 1913; asst. supt., surveys, Jan., 1919; asst. supt., trig. surveys, F.M.S., Nov., 1922; asst. supt., rev. surveys, Sept., 1924; ag. supt., surveys, Johore, Jan. 1931; do., Kedah, Oct., 1932; supt., surveys, F.M.S., and S.S., June, 1933.

STIGAND, ALMAR GORDON.—Ed. Regt., 2nd clk. to res. comsnr. for Bech. Prot., Mafeking, 1898; 1st clk. and registr. to comsnr., 1899; served in town guard during siege of Mafeking, Oct., 1899 to May, 1900; clk. and clk. to asst. comsnr., Gaberones, 1902; Bech. Prot., 1903; ag. asst. comsnr., Jan. to Feb., 1904; 4th Dec., 1905 to 28th Feb., 1906. d 11th May, 1906 to 29th Aug., 1906 ; asst. s. mag. for Southern dist., Bechuanaland Prot., th Jan., 1907 ; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law am., 1909 ; rea. mag., N'gamiland, 1910 ; hon. spr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1914 ; awarded "1914-15" wr for services as mily. intell. offr., German W. Africa Campaign ; lieut. R.W. Kent Regt., 16 ; served B.E.F., France ; attd., intell. I.E.F., sly, Oct., 1917 ; ment. in desps. ; temp. capt., n.. 1919 ; mil. control offr., Italy, to Nov., 19 ; capt., R.W. Kent Regt. R. of O., Mar., 23 ; awarded Murchison grant by R.G.S., May, 23 for surveys and map of N'gamiland ; res. ax., Kweneng dist., Jan., 1924.
reason: award 'Murchison grant by R.G.S.' year not in source

source_text: STONE, ROBERT S., M.D., L.R.C.P. Lond., M.R.C.S.—Formerly house surgeon at St. Bartholomew's hospital, London; assistant surgeon and resident surgeon, civil hospital, Mauritius, Dec., 1864; government medical officer, vaccinator, and commissioner in lunacy for the district of Flacq, 1870 to 1879; was chairman of the local board of health of Flacq until its abolition in 1874; medical officer of the immigration department, Mar., 1879; member of the commission to frame regulations under the labour ordinance of 1878; was acting chief medical officer; president general board of health, member central board of commissioners in lunacy, and member Port Louis prison committee from May, 1881, to Mar., 1882; member of sanitary commission, Feb., 1882; member of board of examiners for the colonial diploma in pharmacy, May, 1882; member of central prison site committee, May, 1882; member of committee for dividing Mauritius into registration areas, May, 1882; member of and reporter to committee of inquiry on emigrant ships, 1884-5.

Lond., M.R.C.S.—Formerly house surgeon at St. Bartholomew's hospital, London; assistant surgeon and resident surgeon, civil hospital, Mauritius, Dec., 1864; government medical officer, vaccinator, and commissioner in lunacy for the district of Flacq, 1870 to 1879; was chairman of the local board of health of Flacq until its abolition in 1874; medical officer of the immigration department, Mar., 1879; member of the commission to frame regulations under the labour ordinance of 1878; was acting chief medical officer; president general board of health, member central board of commissioners in lunacy, and member Port Louis prison committee from May, 1881, to Mar., 1882; member of sanitary commission, Feb., 1882; member of board of examiners for the colonial diploma in pharmacy, May, 1882; member of central prison site committee, May, 1882; member of committee for dividing Mauritius into registration areas, May, 1882; member of and reporter to committee of inquiry on emigrant ships, 1884-5.

flagged_value.position: member of and reporter to committee of inquiry on emigrant ships

source_text: STOCKDALE, Sir Frank Arthur, G.C.M.G. (1945), K.C.M.G. (1937), C.M.G. (1932), C.B.E. (1925).—b. 1883; ed. Wisbech Gram. Sch., Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, M.A.; mycol. and lec. in agric. sci., Imp. Dept. of Agric. for W.I., 1905; asst. dir., dept. of sci. and agric., and gov. botanist, Br. Guiana, 1908; dir. of agric., Maur., 1912; registr. co-op. cred. soc., 1913; official mem. of coun. of gov., 1913; dir. of agric. and registr. of co-op. soc., Ceylon, 1916–29; M.L.C., Ceylon, 1921–29; mem., coun. Ceylon Univ. Coll., 1921–29; mem., bd. of management Tea Research Inst., and chmn. of exec. comtee., Ceylon Rubber Research Scheme; agric. advsr. to S. of S., 1930–40; comptrlr. for dev. and welfare in W.I., 1940–45; advr. on dev. planning, C.O., 1945; dep. chmn., col. dev. corpn., 1947; vice chmn., Col. Adv. Council of Agric. and Animal Health, 1929–40; chmn., E.A. Agric. Confce., 1931; chmn., Col. Directors of Agric. Confces., 1931 and 1937; vice chmn., W.I. Fruit and Veg. Confce., 1933; chmn., Imp. Inst. Adv. Coun. on Plant and Animal products, 1935–40; co-chmn. of Anglo-American Caribbean Comsn., 1942–45; chmn., W.I. Confce., 1944; travelled extensively; author of reports and articles relating to trop. agric. and co-operation; editor of Tropical Agriculturist , 1916–28.
flagged_value: Imp. department of agriculture for W.I.

source_text: STORM, WILLIAM THORBURN. —B. 1881; Imp. post office, 1896-97; asst., Cape of Good Hope P.O., June, 1901; survr.'s staff, N. dist., Dec., 1901; engnr. in ch.'s staff, Nov., 1902; postmr., Feb., 1904; postmr., N.W. Rhodesia, 1909-10; prov. postmr., S. Nigeria, Aug., 1912; survr., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; divnl. survr., 1916; senr. survr., Jan., 1920; ag. dep., P.M.G., various occasions between Jan., 1918 and July 1920; P.M.G., Tanganyika Territory, 8th Oct., 1920; mem. legis. coun., July, 1926; P.M.G., N. Rhodesia, 1931; M.L.C., 1932-33.
SULLIVAN, C. A.—b. 1917; ed. Sacred Heart Sch.; mil. serv., 1935–45, R.A.F.; clk., Ken., 1946; acctnt., treasury, 1953; chief. acctnt., min. of agric., 1956. (Ken. Govt. service.)
SUTHERLAND-GREAME, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1882; dep. loco. supt., F.M.S. Rlys., 1915-24; loco. supt., F.M.S. Rlys., 1924.

(1934), M.A., Aberdeen University.—Science mast., Glen Urquhart H.G. schl., 1903-5; asst. mast., Campbelltown Gram. schl., 1905-6; asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1906; lecturer in biology, Hong Kong Coll. of med., 1909-10; ag. asst. mast., pol. schl., 1909 and 1912; headmast., Victoria British schl., 1913; lieut., R.G.A., on active serv., 1914-19; asst. mast., cls. 1, Jan., 1920; headmast., Indian schl., 1920; ag. inspr., English schls., Feb. to Nov., 1925 and Mar., 1928; J.P., 1928; inspr. English schls., Aug., 1930.

TANNER, COL. (Temp. Brig.-Gen.) WILLIAM ERNEST COLLINS, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1875; lieut., Natal Carbineers, 1899; ent. Natal civ. serv., 1899; served S.A. War, 1899-1902 (Queen's med., 4 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); lieut., dist. adjt., Natal Militia permnt. staff, 1903; capt., 1903; dist. offr., ditto, 1904; sec., Natal defence comtee., 1905; served Natal native rebellion, 1906; med. with clasp; staff offr., hdqrs., Natal Militia, 1907; major, ditto, 1910; grad., Staff Coll., Camberley, 1910; major, Permnt. Force (staff), Union Defence Forces, 1912; temp. lt.-col., 1914; brev. lt.-col., Permnt. Force (staff), 1915; lt.-col. comdg. 2nd S.A. Infy., Union Imp. Serv. Cont. (Overseas), 1915; brig.-comdr. (temp. brig.-gen.), 8th Inf. Bde., 1917; bde.-comdr., S.A. Inf. Bde., 1918; ret. with rank of brig.-gen. in Imp. Army, 1919; served in Great War, 1914-18, G.S.W. Africa, 1914-15, Egypt, 1916, and France, 1916-18; wounded, Delville Wood; ment. in despa. six times; "1914-15" Star, Br. War Med., Victory Med., Croix d'Offr., Ordre de Leopold and Croix de Guerre (Belgium), Croix de Chevalier, Legion d'Honneur (France); dist. staff offr., No. 1 mily. dist., and O.C. base, Capetown, 1919; ag. adjt.-gen., defence hdqrs., 1919; substantive lt.-col. (staff offr., 1st grade), Permt. Force (staff), 1919; adjt.-gen. and temp. col., Nov., 1919; ag. comdt., S.A. Mil. Coll. and O.C., troops, Roberts Heights, 1920; O.C., Cape Peninsula garrison, 1921; substantive col., 1921; temp. brig.-gen., 1921; dist. staff offr., No. 1 mily. dist. (Cape), in addn. to other duties, 1921; posted S.A. Staff Corps, 1923; adjt.-gen., defence hdqrs., 1925.

TAYLOR, Robert Mackinlay, C.B.E. (1956)—b. 1912; ed. Plymouth Coll., Hele's Sch., Exeter, and Univ. Coll., Exeter; mil. serv., 1939–47, col.; asst. comsnr., nat. savings comtee., U.K.; econ. advr., Fiji (secon.), 1947; fin. sec., Fiji, 1948; N. Rhod., 1952; secon. fedl. govt., Rhod. and Nyasa., 1953; author of A Social Survey of Plymouth , 1938.

TAYLOR, W. T., C.M.G. (1895).—Collr. of customs and excise, Larnaka, 1879; ch. collr. of customs, Cyprus, 1882; ag. rec.-gen., Nov., 1883, to Feb., 1881; ag. comsnr., Larnaka, in addition to his own duties, 1883 and 1885; rec.-gen., ch. collr. customs and excise, 1891; audr.-gen., Ceylon, 1895, and at intervals to Dec., 1896; ag. col. sec. 1895 to Mar., 1896, and Mar., 1899.

THOMPSON, AUGUSTUS WM. — Asst. clk. of ct., G. Coast, 1873; clk. to Queen's advoc., 1874-5; dep. registr., cent. prov., 1877; ch. registr., 1880; postmr., Cape Coast, Mar. to Aug., 1884; registr. and interp., W. prov., 1884; registr. of deeds and taxing master, W. prov.; dist. comsur., G. C. Col. May, 1889.

THOMPSON, JOHN JAMES MILLAR.—Admitted to practice at the Bahama bar, Easter term, 1862; appointed notary public same year; elected member of the house of assembly, 1866, and on the dissolution of that body in 1869 was re-elected; appointed master in chancery 1869, resigned 1873; admitted to practice at the Turks and Caicos Islands' bar, 1873; commissioned as a notary public; appointed a justice of the peace of the colony, provisional Queen's advocate and escheator general, Oct. 1873; confirmed, 1874; director of public bank, 1874.

THOMSON, Col. G. B., C.B.E. (1962), M.B.E.—b. 1906; ed. Balfron High Sch. and Tech. Coll., Glasgow; mil. serv., 1940–46, col. (desps. twice); allied occupation, Germany, 1946–51; asst., ch. trans. div. (rail bch.) Berlin and Frankfurt, 1952–53; traffic man., trans. and harbours dept., B. Guiana, 1953; gen. man., 1954–62.

THOMSON, FRANK DAVID, B.A., C.M.G., (1920).—B. 1877; ed. High Schl., Napier and Canterbury and Victoria Colls., N.Z.; cadet. educn. dept., 1894; priv. sec. to various ministers since 1901; sec. to prime min., since 1912; clk. of exec. coun. and sec. of cabinet since 1919; permt. head, prime minister's dept., 1926; mem. of secretariat, Br. Empire delegation, Peace Confce., Paris, 1919; mem., Imp. Confce. secretariat, 1917, 1918, 1921, 1923, 1926 and 1930.

THOMSON, JOHN JAMES MILLAR.—Admitted to practice at the Bahama bar, Easter term, 1862; appointed notary public same year; elected member of the house of assembly, 1866, and on the dissolution of that body in 1869 was re-elected; appointed master in chancery 1869, resigned 1873; admitted to practice at the Turks and Caicos Islands' bar, 1873; commissioned as a notary public; appointed a justice of the peace of the colony, provisional Queen's advocate and escheator general, Oct. 1873; confirmed, 1874; director of public bank, 1874; acted as judge of the supreme court and ordinary, from Dec., 1877, to March, 1878.

TOLMIE, HON. SIMON FRASER.—B. 1867; ed. Victoria High Schl., Ontario, and Vet. Coll., Toronto; farmer and breeder of live stock; pres., Dominion Holstein Breeders, B. Columbia Vet. Assoc., B. Columbia Holstein Breeders; chf. inspr. of health of animals branch, B. Columbia; B. C. representative on live stock comsn.; elec. of Canadian H. of C., g. e., 1917 and 1921; min. to agric. in fed. govt., Aug., 1919; resig. portfolio on defeat of admstn., in general election, Dec., 1921; premier, Br. Columbia, Aug., 1928.

TRUTER, SIR THEODORUS (1924), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 187 Schl., George; Normal Coll., C Coll., Rondebosch; Cape civ chief examr., accts., 1895; lieut. 1899-1902 (med. and four c Pretoria, Apr., 1901; mem. mag., Ermelo, Dec., 1904; ma 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal, S. African pol., Oct., 1910 Africa; ret., 1928.

TURNER, LEWIS JAMES BARNETSON.—B. 1880; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon dragoons, Oct., 1908; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Gampola, Nov., 1911; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., May, 1913; asst. comsnr. of excise, Nov., 1913; asst. comsnr., S. Div., Jan., 1914.

TURNER, LEWIS JAMES BARNETSON. —B. 1885. ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ; ser., Oct., 1908; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Gampola, Nov., 1911; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., May, 1913; asst. comsnr. of excise, Nov., 1913; asst. comsnr., S. Div., Jan., 1914; ditto, N.W. Div., Aug., 1916; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, in addition to his own duties, Oct., 1917; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1918; asst. comsnr. of excise, N.W. divn., and asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Aug., 1919; ag. asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1919; supt. of census, July, 1920; dir. of statistics in addn., Sept., 1920; designation changed to dir., statistics and office systems, Oct., 1926; regisar.-gen., June, 1931.

TWOSE, LAWRENCE RENDLE.—B. 1891; clk. to mag., dist. A, 1915; sec. to fire brig. comsnr., 1916; lieut., H.M. forces, 1916-19; ag. A.D.C. to govt., Leeward Is., on occasion of visit of Prince of Wales, 1920; clk. to comsnr., clk., couns., dep. coroner, educn. dist. offr., Montserrat, 1920; J.P., chmn., prison justices, 1924; mag., coroner, regisar.-gen., sup. ct., 1924; M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1925.

VELLACOTT, PHILIP NORTHCOTT, M.B. Lond. (hons. in for. and obst. med.), 1895; B.S., 1896; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896; F.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1901.—House surg., res. obstetric and clin. asst., Guy's hosp.; asst. curator of museum, St. George's hosp.; civ. surg., S. African field force, 1901 to 1902; capt., S.A. Constaby., 6th June, 1902; transfd. to O.R.C. govt. serv. as res. med. offr., National hosp., Bloemfontein, 1st Dec., 1905; fellow of R. Inst. of pub. health; deleg. from O.R.C. to 2nd Internat. Congress on School Hygiene.

VELLACOTT, PHILIP NORTHCOTT, M.B. Lond. (hons. in for. and obst. med.), 1895; B.S., 1896; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896; F.R.C.S. Congress on School Hygiene.

VELLACOTT, PHILIP NORTHCOTT, M.B. Lond. (hons. in for. and obst. med.), 1895; B.S., 1896; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896; F.R.C.S. Congress on School Hygiene.

VILJOEN, WILLEM JACOBUS, M.A., L.N.C., Phil. D.—B. 1869; ed. at Normal Coll., Capetown, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and S. African Coll., Capetown; apptd. to staff, Normal Training Coll., Capetown, 1889; after graduation studied at Univs. of Amsterdam, Leyden, Strasbourg, and in 1894 took degree of Phil. D. (in modern languages and comparative philology); apptd. prof. of modern languages, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Sept., 1894; elected mem. of coun. of Univ. of C. of G.H., 1897; mem. of De Mattschappij der Nederlandsche Letteren in Leiden, 1900; dir. of educn., O.F.S. Prov., Apl., 1910; chmn., Grey Univ. Coll. coun., 1911; represented Union of S. Africa at Impl. Educn. Confce., in London, June, 1911; mem. of Univ. Statutes Comsn. and elec. pro.-vice-chancellor of Univ. of Cape of Good Hope, 1916; supt. gen. of educn., Cape Prov., 1st July, 1918; mem. of coun., Huguenot Univ. Coll., Wellington, 1919; first vice-chancellor, Univ. of S. Africa, 1918-22; joint editor of "Nederlands Woordeboek voor Zuid-Afrika," and "English-Dutch and Dutch-English Dictionary" (in the simplified spelling).

VILJOEN, WILLEM JACOBUS, M.A., L.N.C., Phil. D. — B. 1869; ed. at Normal Coll., Capetown, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and S. African Coll., Capetown; apptd. to staff, Normal Training Coll., Capetown, 1889; after graduation studied at Univ. of Amsterdam, Leyden, Strasbourg, and in 1894 took degree of Phil. D. (in modern languages and comparative philology); prof. mod. languages and hist., Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, 1895-1903; prof. of Dutch (lang. and lit.) ditto, now Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1903-10; elec. mem. of coun. of C. of G.H. Univ., 1897; mem., Maatschappij der Nederlandse, Leiden, 1900; rep. Cape Univ. at quinquennial of Leipzig Univ., 1909; dir., educn., O.F.S., 1910-18; mem. and chmn., of Grey Univ. Coll. coun., Bloemfontein, 1911-18; rep. Union of S. Africa at Impl. Educn. Confce., in London, 1911 and 1923; first vice-chan., Univ. of S. Africa, 1918; supt. gen. of educn., Cape Prov., 1918; mem., coun., Huguenot Univ. Coll., Wellington, 1919; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1923; author of several standard wks. on Dutch language and simplified orthography.

WADE, Sir Armigel de Vins, Kt. (1937), C.M.G. (1935), O.B.E. (1931).—b. 1880; ed. Lancing Coll. (exhibtnr.) and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar.), M.A.; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A. Prot., Mar., 1912; ag. dep. ch. native comsnr., Ken., 1926; prin. asst., native affrs., dept. Jan., 1928; ch. native comsnr., 1932; ag. ch. sec., 1933; ch. sec., 1934; ag. gov., 1935 and 1936; ret., 1939.

WALKER, George Blyth, C.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H.—b. 1899 ; ed. Grove Acad., Broughty Ferry and Edin. Univ. ; on mil. serv., 1917–18 ; med. offr., Nig., 1922 ; M.O.H., 1924 ; senr. health offr., 1927 ; dep. dir., health serv., 1937 ; D.M.S., 1946.

WALKER, CAPT. REGINALD DAVID, M.C., A.R.C.S., D.I.C., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.T., Chtd. civ. engnr.—B. 1892; ed. R. Coll. Science, and City and Guilds Engngr. Coll., Whitworth Exhibr., 1913; field cov., R.E., France, Salonika, Egypt, 1915-18; capt., 1917; R.O.D., R.E., Egypt, 1918-20; asst. engnr., F.M.S. Rlys., 1920; ag. dist. engnr., 1923; dist. engnr., grade II, 1925; mtnce. asst. engnr., ways and wks., 1926 and 1932; dist. engnr., grade I, 1929; ag. sec., gen. man., F.M S. rlys., 1932; ag. estate and survey offr., F.M.S.R., 1933; man. and ch. engnr., Kowloon-Canton Rly., Hong Kong., 1934; maj., H.K. Vol. Defence Corps, 1935.

WALKER, CAPT. REGINALD DAVID, M.C., A.R.C.S., D.I.C., M.Inst.C.E., M.Inst.T., Chtd. civ. engnr.—B. 1892; ed. R. Coll. Science, and City and Guilds Engngr. WALTON, GEORGE O'DONNELL.—Matriculated a student of Lond. Univ., June, 1891; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 17th Nov., 1893; ag. on several occasions as pol. mag., Barbados; judge of the petty debt court of Bridgetown; judge of the asst. ct. of appeal; ag. pol. mag., Bridgetown and dist. "A" from 21st July, 1902, to 14th Feb., 1903; pol. mag., dist. "F." 9th Dec., 1902; ag. chief just., St. Lucia, 18th May to 29th Oct., 1908.
