TEVENSON, W. B.—B. 1874; entd. Bahamas ser., 1903; Out Island comsnr. (4th div.), 19.

O'FARRELL, PATRICK. — Army wt. offr., ret. Mar., 1892; 20½ years' ser.; med. for Afghan campaign, 1st and 2nd phase, 1878-80; Hythe certif. and 1st cl. certif. of educn.; trained at Portland for prison ser.; prison offr., Antigua, 1892; keeper of gaol, St. Kitts, Jan., 1893; spec. ser., Dominica gaol, Feb.-Mar., 1894; ag. inspr. of pol., St. Kitts, Apr., 1895; inspr. of pol., Dec., 1895; recd. thanks of S. of S. for ser. after hurricane, 1899; J.P., St. Kitts, Mar., 1900; and visiting just. to gaol, May, 1900; instr., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, May, 1902; chmn., telephone bd., Apr., 1903, to Sept., 1904, and from May, 1906, to Aug., 1907; mem. Basse-terre town bd., July, 1903; ag. adjut., St. Kitts def. force, Apr., 1903, to Sept., 1904, and May, 1906, to Aug., 1907; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, Jan., 1904; capt., St. Kitts-Nevis def. force, Aug., 1904; inspr. of pol., Dominica, and mil. instr. to def. reserve, Aug., 1907; offr. in charge pris. discipline, Dominica, Oct., 1907; J.P., Dominica, 1907; mem. Roseau town bd., Feb., 1909.

EVENS, PERCIVAL, Assoc. M.I.C.E.—B. ; asst. engnr. govt. rlys., Trinidad, 1874; engnr., Couva extension rly., 1878; res. r. in charge of construction, San Fernando Guaiacara rlys., 1879; 1st asst. engnr., P.W. , Jan., 1885; has acted as asst. dir. of P.W., , 6, 8, 9, and 90; engnr., N. div., P.W.D., , 1892; 1st engnr. in charge of rds. and bdges., V.D., Jan., 1894; engnr. in charge of rds. and res, rds. and bdges. dept., Jan., 1895; asst. dir. pub. wks., and sen. div. engnr., Jan., 1897; acted as D.P.W. with seat in legis. coun., 4, 5, 8, 9, 1900, 2, 5, 6, and 7.

EVENS, PERCIVAL, Assoc. M.I.C.E.—B. ; asst. engnr. govt. rlys., Trinidad, 1874; engnr., Couva extension rly., 1878; res. r. in charge of construction, San Fernando Guaiacara rlys., 1879; 1st asst. engnr., P.W. , Jan., 1885; has acted as asst. dir. of P.W., , 6, 8, 9, and 90; engnr., N. div., P.W.D., , 1892; 1st engnr. in charge of rds. and bdges., V.D., Jan., 1894; engnr. in charge of rds. and res, rds. and bdges. dept., Jan., 1895; asst. dir. pub. wks., and sen. div. engnr., Jan., 1897; acted as D.P.W. with seat in legis. coun., 4, 5, 8, 9, 1900, 2, 5, 6, and 7.
VERNON, J.—Employed in rly. dept., N.S. Wales; chief acctnt. in treasury.; auditor gen. N.S.W., and deputy auditor gen. in N.S.W. for Australian Commonwealth, since Nov., 1902.

flagged_value.position: deputy auditor general in N.S.W. for Australian Commonwealth

source_text: BLAINE, HERBERT FRANCIS, K.C. —Ed. at Harrow and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; called to Bar (In. Temp.), 1881; admitted advoc., sup. ct. of Cape, 1881; M.A., 1889; twice ag. sol.-gen. East. dists. ct.; att.-gen., O.R.C., May, 1901; mem. I.C.C., 1903; ag. atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1905, and 1906-7; mem. exec. and legis. couns.

CHERRY, JAS. WM.—Ed. at Bedford Modern schl.; apptd. asst. ch. clk. and acctnt., P.W.D., G. Coast. 9th July, 1898; ch. clk. and acctnt., 1st Jan., 1900; offl. mem. Accra town coun., 1898, 1899, 1900 and 1901; chrmn. streets and roads comtee.; apptd. 2nd lieut. G. C. rifle volunteers, 16th Nov., 1898; lieut. 17th Aug., 1900; acted adjutant, 3rd May to 30th June, 1899, and 26th June, 1900, to 1st June, 1901; apptd. capt., 12th Oct., 1901; ag. offr. commanding, Dec., 1901, to July, 1902; certificate, army form, E 511, Chelsea barracks, Oct., 1901.

McEWAN, THOS. SMITH.—Assoc. M.I.C.E.; asst. construction engnr., Cape govt. rlwys., 1874; maintenance engnr., 1877; dist. engnr., 1880; res. engnr., Bethulie junction, Jan., 1891; East London, 1st Jan., 1893; Port Elizabeth, 1st May, 1894; res. engnr., western system, and dist. engnr., Dist. No. 1, 13th July, 1896; res. engnr., Cape Town, 1st July, 1897; ch. res. engnr., Port Elizabeth, 1st Apl., 1899; asst. engnr. in-chief, 15th Mar., 1901; asst. gen. man. of rlwys., 16th May, 1901; gen. man., 12th Aug., 1902.

MORGAN, R. F.—Admitted an advocate of the supreme court of Ceylon, 1846; district judge of Colombo, Ceylon, Oct. 1856; acting puisne judge of the supreme court in that colony, 1857; called to the English bar at Lincoln's Inn, Nov. 1858; acting Queen's advocate, 1857; acting senior puisne judge, 1860; district judge of Colombo, 1860; acting Queen's advocate, April, 1861; resumed duties as district judge, Colombo, Nov. 1861; acting deputy Queen's advocate for the island; appointed Queen's advocate Jan. 1, 1863; and allowed private practice; is one of the law officers of the crown in the colony.

REEVE-TUCKER, W. R.—Lieut. D.C.O. Hants and Isle of Wight Arty., 1885; capt., 1890; hon. maj., 1900; attd. three yrs., 1887-8-9, as subaltern to service batteries, Roy. Arty.; asst. inspr. Gold Coast Hausas, 1891; asst. inspr. Lagos Hausas, 1892; inspr., 1896; commanded Lagos Hausas, Ashanti expedn., 1895-6; wing offr., Malay States Guides, 1896; comsnr. of police, Lagos, 1898; travelling comsnr., 1899.

REEVE-TUCKER, W. R.—Lieut. 1st Vol. Batt. East Kent Regt., 1883; lieut. D.C.O. Hants and Isle of Wight Arty. Mil., 1885; ten months embodiment serv., 1885-6; capt., 1890; hon. maj., 1900; major, 1903; attd. three yrs., 1887-8-9, as subaltern to service batteries, Roy. Arty., 1887-8-9; asst. inspr. Gold Coast Hausas, 1891; asst. inspr. Lagos Hausas, 1892; ag. dist. comsnr., Leckie, Lagos, 1893; inspr., Lagos constab., 1896; commanded Lagos Hausas, Ashanti expdn. (ment. in desp., star), 1895-6; wing offr., Malay States Guides, 1896-7; ag. supt., central prison, Perak, 1897; comsnr. of pol., Lagos, 1898; ag. A.D.C. to ag. gov., Lagos, 1898; trav. comsnr., Lagos, 1899; ag. res. Ibadan, 1905.

TRANCHELL, MAJOR E. F.—Cadet Royal Military College, Sandhurst; entered the army in 1848; served 28 years in the Ceylon rifle regiment, during which time he served with a detachment of his regiment in China, 1850-51; held the appointment of staff officer of the Kandian district, 1859-61; commandant of Jaffna, 1864-66; commandant of Kandy, 1866-68; commanded the troops at Labuna 1868-69; commandant of Trincomalee, 1870-71; selected for special service connected with recruiting for the regiment, 1858-59, and visited the Straits Settlements, Siam, Brunei, Sarawak, and other parts of Borneo; superintendent of police, Mar., 1871; acting inspector-general, April, 1882, to May, 1883, and from Mar. to Nov., 1886.

WERRY, CAPT. FRANCIS EDWARD.—B. 1873; ed. at I. of Wight Coll. and Uppingham schl.; capt., 4th batt. Essex regt.; dist. comsnr., Lagos, July, 1902; ag. comsnr. of pol., Aug. to Oct., 1902; ag. trav. comsnr., N.E. dist., Jan. to June, 1903, and July, 1904, to Feb., 1905; ag. asst. col. sec., June to Aug., 1903; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Jan. to June, 1904; ag. asst. res., Ibadan, Aug. to Sept., 1905; dist. comsnr., Ondo, Oct., 1905, to Oct., 1906, and from May, 1907.

WERRY, CAPT. FRANCIS EDWARD.—B. 1873; ed. at I. of Wight Coll. and Uppingham schl.; capt., 4th batt. Essex regt.; dist. comsnr., Lagos, July, 1902; ag. comsnr. of pol., Aug. to Oct., 1902; ag. trav. comsnr., N.E. dist., Jan. to June, 1903, and July, 1904, to Feb., 1905; ag. asst. col. sec., June to Aug., 1903; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Jan. to June, 1904; ag. asst. res., Ibadan, Aug. to Sept., 1905; dist. comsnr., Ondo, Oct., 1905, to Oct., 1906, and from May, 1907.

ASHBURNER, E.—B. 1877; served vol. coy., King's Own Royal Lancaster Regt., 1900-01; clk., law dept., 1901; recr., rev., Klerksdorp, 1904; ditto, Krugersdorp, 1910; prin. clk., Joh'burg, 1913; ditto, Pretoria, 1917; survr., inland rev.; Pietermaritzburg, 1924; recr., rev., Pretoria, 1928; ch. rev. offr., inland rev. dept., Pretoria, 1930; recr., rev., Durban. 1932.

BAYES, HAROLD NEWTON, M.I.R.S.E.—B. 1885; signal inspr., Burma, May, 1907-June, 1910; rly. and signal engr., Australia, Apr., 1912-Sept., 1913; signal and tel. inspr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1913; asst. signal engr., F.M.S. Rlys., 1917; district signal engr., grade II, Jan. 1922; grade I, Jan., 1926; dist. signal engr., Tampin, Apr., 1926; dist. signal and tel. engr., Gemas, Aug., 1928.

CORBETT, Miss A. W.—b. 1915; nursing sister, Aden, 1949; Uga., 1954–63. (Uga. Govt. service.)

Brig., France, M.C. Sept., 1914-July, 1919; S. African Rlys., July, 1919; supt. line, Kenya and Uganda Rly., Sept., 1922; ditto, Nigerian Rly., Sept., 1925; dep. gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1931; gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., May, 1933.

GOLDENHUYS, FRANS EDUARD.—B. 1889; B.A., 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; studied in U.S.A. at Univ. of Cornell, Wisconsin, Illinois and California; B.Sc., Cornell Univ., 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of nativ. study and agrl. science, Grey Coll. Schl., 1913; edr., "Die Landbou-weekblad," Bloemfontein S.A., 1919; first ch., divn. of agrl. economics and marketing, dept. of agr., S.A., 1925; under sec. for agr., 1926; dir., forestry, 1931; comm. coun., Rome, Oct., 1934.

PARSONS, CUTHBERT LEO, M.I.R.S.E.—B. 1884 ; asst. signal and teleg. engnr., sp. services, F.M.S. Rlys., Sept., 1914 ; dist. signal engnr., grade II ; grade I, Sept., 1923 ; dist. signal and teleg. engnr., grade I, Aug., 1928 ; ditto, F.M.S. Rlys., attd. to head office, K. Lumpur, June, 1930.

JOHN, K.C.M.G. (1879).—Born 1835; entered Canadian Parliament, 1863; sworn of the privy council, and was minister of finance, 7th Nov., 1873, until the resignation of the Mackenzie cabinet, Oct., 1878.

BURFORD-HANCOCK, SIR HENRY JAMES, KNT., 1882. — Educated at Eton; served in her Majesty's 45th regiment (Sherwood Foresters), and a captain in the Kent artillery militia; called to the bar, Inner Temple, January, 1866; received medal from H.I.M. Napoleon III, for "Treatise on International Fishery Laws," 1866; district judge in Jamaica, 1st May, 1876; appointed to Falmouth district, 10th August, 1876; acted, in addition, as judge of Montego Bay district, May, 1877, and as judge of Mandeville district, March, 1878; attorney-general of the Leeward Islands, June, 1878; chancellor of the diocese of Antigua, Oct., 1878; acted as chief justice in conjunction with the office of attorney-general from 8th April, 1879, to 16th March, 1880; chief justice of the Leeward Islands, 1880; administered the government-in-chief of the Leeward Islands, Nov., 1881; chief justice of Gibraltar, 1882.

KNIGHT, ALWIN TRIGGS.—B. 1887; statn. mast., Ipoh, July, 1913; ditto, K. Lumpur, Dec., 1913; ditto, Prai, Jan., 1914; traffic inspr., Turnpat, Jan., 1915; sec. to Siamese State Rlys., Sept., 1917; asst. traffic man., F.M.S. Rlys., Jan., 1918; asst. traffic man., S'pore, May, 1931.

JERVOIS, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR WILLIAM FRANCIS DRUMMOND, G.C.M.G. (1878), (K.C.M.G., 1874), C.B., R.E.—Served at the Cape of Good Hope from April, 1841, until September, 1848, of which time he was six years on the frontier and in Kaffraria. In 1842 he was appointed to the staff as brigade-major to a force of all arms sent on an expedition against the Dutch Boers; served in Kaffir war of 1846-7, and made map of British Kaffraria, since used both for military and civil purposes. From 1852 to 1855 was specially employed at Alderney in designing and executing the fortifications of that island; from 1856 to 1875 was on the staff of the war office, first as assistant inspector-general of fortifications, and subsequently as deputy-director of fortifications. From 1857 to 1875 was secretary of the committee on the defence of the empire, and in 1859 secretary to the royal commission on national defences; whilst in war office, carried out fortifications at Portsmouth, Plymouth, Pembroke, Portland, the Thames, Medway, and Cork; in 1863 was nominated a civil C.B.; in 1863 was sent to Canada, New Brunswick, Nova Scotia and Bermuda, to report on the defences of those provinces; and again in 1864, to confer with the Canadian government as to the defences of the dominion. His proposals were approved by the imperial and colonial authorities, and those for Bermuda and Halifax have been adopted and carried out; in 1865 was sent to Malta, and in 1866 to Gibraltar, to prepare projects for the improvement of the defences at those fortresses. He again visited Halifax and Bermuda in 1869, and made further reports on the defences; in 1871-2 was employed by the government of India to inspect and report upon the defences of Aden, Perim, Bombay, and the Hooghly; and submitted reports to the secretary of state for India in council respecting the defences of those places. He also visited British Burmah, and subsequently submitted reports with regard to the defences of Rangoon and Moulmein. Governor of the Straits Settlements, 1875-7; successfully quelled insurrection of Malays in States of Perak and Sungei Ujong; submitted plan for government of protected Malay States, which has resulted in a career of prosperity therein; was selected by H.M.'s government to proceed to the Australian colonies to advise the respective governments regarding a scheme of defence for the Australian ports; governor, New Zealand, Nov., 1882.

MOORE, F. C.—b. 1907 ; ed. Victoria Univ. Coll., N.Z. ; mil. serv., 1941–46 ; temp. admin. offr., B.S.I.P., 1943 ; inspr., police, 1946 ; supt., police and prisons, G. and E.I. Col., 1951.

PEEL, EDMUND.—Lieut. R.A., 1876 to 1883; served through the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal); assistant inspector, Gold Coast constabulary, 1883; on special service, Sierra Leone, on the staff of Sir S. Rowe, 1885, and again in 1886; inspector-general of constabulary, Lagos, 1886; ditto, Jamaica, 1891.
POWER, W. M. L.—Capt., 4th Batt., Liverpool Regt.; asst. supt. of pol., S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1903.

SMYTH, GENERAL SIR EDWARD SELBY, K.C.M.G. (1877).—Served as brigade-major to the forces in the Southern Concan and Sawant Warree country during the campaign of 1844-45, and was present at the attack and capture of several strong stockades, in the operations before the mountain forts of Monohur, and at their final assault; at the forcing of the Kirwattee Pass, and subsequent occupation of the country below the Ghats; in the Kaffir war of 1851-52 (medal), and mentioned in general orders for his conduct in command of a column in action in the Fish River Bush—brevet major; with the expedition north of the Orange River in 1852-53; afterwards Dy. A.-Q.-M.-Gen. of the 2nd division; and subsequently as D.-Adj.-Gen. and D.-Q.-M.-Gen. to the forces in South Africa from [January, 1854, to July, 1860; commanded the troops, Mauritius, from 13th April, 1870; assumed the administration of the government, 3rd June, 1870; and from 19th Aug. till 29th Sept., 1871, acting secretary to the government in the eastern provinces, Cape of Good Hope, in 1859, when Quartermaster-general of the army; inspector-general of militia in Ireland from 1861 till October, 1867; a special magistrate for the county and city of Dublin, 1867; appointed to command the Canadian militia, 1874 till 1880; employed by the Canadian government to conduct an expedition across the North-West Territories—the Rocky Mountains, and British Columbia, to Vancouver's Island, in 1875, to make a general reconnaissance and to report on the defences of Esquimalt and Victoria. Received a minute conveying the thanks of the Governor-General in Council for preserving the peace in Montreal with a large force of troops in July, 1878; colonel of the Seaforth Highlanders (the Duke of Albany's), Mar., 1881; promoted general in 1882; J.P. for Middlesex and Westminster, Feb., 1884; reserve list of general officers, 1885.

TILLEY, SIR SAMUEL LEONARD, K.C.M.G. (1879), C.B. (1867).—Born 1818; sat in legislative assembly of New Brunswick almost uninterruptedly from 1854 to the union of 1867, when returned to the house of commons; was a member of the executive council, N.B. from November, 1854, to May, 1856; from July, 1857, to March, 1865; and again from April, 1866, until the union; during which several periods he held the office of provincial secretary of that province; and from March, 1861, to March, 1865, was leader of the government; was leader of the liberal party in New Brunswick for a lengthened period, and was a delegate to England on several occasions to confer with the imperial government on important public business, notably regarding the union of the British North American colonies and the construction of an intercolonial railway; has also repeatedly served on like missions to the sister provinces; was a delegate to the Charlottetown Union Conference, 1864; to that in Quebec, same year; and to the London colonial conference, to complete terms of union of the British North American provinces, 1866-7. Holds a patent of rank and precedence from Her Majesty as an ex-councillor, New Brunswick; sworn of the privy council, 1st July, 1867, and held the office of minister of customs from that date until 22nd February, 1873, when appointed minister of finance, in which office he remained until appointed lieutenant-governor of New Brunswick, 5th November, same year; re-entered political life, May, 1878, when he resigned the lieut.-governorship of New Brunswick; was elected one of the members for St. John in house of commons; and on the formation of the new administration of Sir John A. Macdonald in October, 1878, accepted office as minister of finance; resigned, 10th Nov., 1885, on appointment for the 2nd time as lieut.-governor of New Brunswick.

FOX, J.—b. 1927; ed. Cathays High Sch.; mil. serv., 1947-49; Br. P.O., 1943-49; techn., gr. II, E.A.P. & T. admin., 1949; gr. I, 1950; asst. engnr., gr. II, 1955; gr. I (old style), 1959; gr. I (new style), 1960; retd., re-aptd., 1963; senr. asst. engnr., 1964.

PORCH, MONTAGU PHIPPEN.—B. 1877; educ. at Bath Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1902; M.A., 1904; served with [the] Yeomanry in S. African war, 1900 [and 1901] naval and [naval] history with P. [the] [Royal] [Navy]. No. [of] [the] 1st [Royal] [Grenadiers]. [Joined] 2nd class [as] sec. to [the] pol. force [of] the Crown [in] 1891; ag. [the] comsnr. [of] [the] [Northern] [Provinces]. [Served as] manager of [the] [Northern] [Provinces]. [Appointed] to Sept., 1893; 3rd clk. [in] H.M. [Customs]. 1st [Class], 1897; ass't [the] 1913. [Served as] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], Jul [y] [1914] (Rodri [guez] and luna [Islands]). [Appointed] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], 1883.—L. [Served as] the physician for [the] [Royal] [Navy] for the 6th [Fleet]. [Served as] the str. for Valparaiso, Med. Assoc. [of] Gyna-col. [Served as] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], Berlin, 1894. [Appointed] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], W. [Served as] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], Dor [set] and excise off [icer] and quara [ntine] treas., I [reland]. [Served as] ag. mag., [the] treas., Don [Drummond] of Dominica [Island]. Apr., 1906, [Appointed] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], town [Treas.], dist. ct [Court], P. A. H [enry]. G. [the] Coast, S [outh]. Jan., 191 [0]. [Served as] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], Oct. to [Nov.], 1910; ag. prov. [the] [Chief] [Inspector], June, 1906 [Appointed] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], 1910, to June, [1912]. [Served as] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], [the] [Chief] [Inspector], Edinburgh [Asst.]. Artill. July, 1894 [Appointed] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], July [1896]. Blvth and W [est] North British Rly. [Appointed] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], John Strain, Gh [ia]. [Served as] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], Gh [ia]. [Appointed] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], 189 [Appointed] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], Admiralty, 18 [Appointed] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], 21st Nov., [1898]. [Served as] [the] [Chief] [Inspector], 14th Feb., 19[01]. medal and three clasps); Egyptian exploration fund with Prof. Flinders Petrie, 1904 and 1905; asst. res., Northern Nigeria, 14th July, 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1st Apl., 1912.

FRASER, ROSE DEMPSTER.—Comptroller of customs and navigation laws, and registrar of shipping, Barbados; entered the imperial customs, as landing-waiter and searcher at Bermuda, 1838; appointed senior searcher, 1839; nominated 8th April 1845, by warrant from governor of Bermuda (the late Sir W. Reid), to survey and report on all public moneys in the colonial treasury; nominated acting landing surveyor, 1848; appointed same year landing waiter and searcher at Bridgetown, Barbadoes; appointed landing waiter and searcher at Kingston, Jamaica, 1850; which appointment was cancelled in the year following, at his request, and on nomination as acting landing surveyor, Barbados; appointed first-class landing waiter, colonial customs, Barbados, 1853; landing surveyor, 1855; and comptroller, &c., 1857.

FRASER, ROSE DEMPSTER.—Comptroller of customs and navigation laws, and registrar of shipping, Barbados; entered the imperial customs, as landing-waiter and searcher at Bermuda, 1838; appointed senior searcher, 1839; nominated 8th April 1845, by warrant from governor of Bermuda (the late Sir W. Reid), to survey and report on all public moneys in the colonial treasury; nominated acting landing surveyor, 1848; appointed same year landing waiter and searcher at Bridgetown, Barbadoes; appointed landing waiter and searcher at Kingston, Jamaica, 1850; which appointment was cancelled in the year following, at his request, and on nomination as acting landing surveyor, Barbados; appointed first-class landing waiter, colonial customs, Barbados, 1853; landing surveyor, 1855; and comptroller, &c., 1857.

HAWKINS, A. CESAR.—Entered the army as ensign in the 1st royals, Feb. 1836, and retired after 12 years service; appointed resident magistrate of the Weenen, in colony of Natal, Sept. 1852, and removed to resident magistracy of the Upper Umcomas division of that colony, Feb. 1855; was a major in a yeomanry cavalry regiment of volunteers called the Natal carbiniers.

THBERT, MORTON.—Ed. Edin. Institn. and W and West of Scot. Tech. Coll.; apprent. to Messrs. Dick, Kerr and Co., Ltd., Lon- 895; electrn. with Glasgow corptn. elec. 1899; asst. engnr. Govan corptn. elec. dept., chief asst. engnr., Oban corptn. elec. dept., electrl. engnr., B. Honduras, 1905; exec. electrl. engnr., P.W.D., B. Honduras, 1909; B.H.T.F., 1917; ag. adjutant, B.H.T.F., 1917 to Jan., 1918; hon. A.D.C. to gov., B. ras, 1919.

MERON, SIR DONALD CHARLES, C.G.M.G. , K.C.M.G. (1926), K.B.E. (1922), C.M.G. —B. 1872; ed. at Rathmines School, Dublin; Br. Guiana civ. ser., 1890; 5th class clk., variat, 1891; 4th ditto, 1895; 3rd class and tch clk., 1895; 2nd class, 1899; priv. to ag. govr., 1896, 1897, 1898 and 1901; st. gov. sec. and clk. of councils, 1900-1901, pal clk., secretariat, 1901; priv. sec. to govr. Newfoundland (with permission of S. of S.) on leave, 1902; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1904; ag. col. sec. in 1904-5-6-7; temp. fd. to S. Nigeria as asst. sec., Jan., 1908; pal asst. sec., 1911; sec. to S. Nigeria liquor inquiry comtee., 1909; ag. prov. comsnr. 1910, 1911 and 1912; ag. col. sec., 27th Feb. 1912, and 5th Sept. to 3rd Oct., 1912; y gov. in 1921-23; cent. sec., Nigeria, 1st 1914; ch. sec. to govt., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. n 1921, 1923 and 1924; gov. and comdr.-in- Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1924; ditto, ia, 1931.

ECHIEL, PERCY HUBERT, C.M.G. (1923). — 875; B.A. Bombay, 1889 (æt. 14); B.A., on, 1894; major schlr., Trin. Coll., Camb., ; 4th wrangler and B.A., Camb., 1897; apptd., compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., 11th Oct., ; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1903; to crown agts. for the colonies, May, 1905; of Fair Wages Advisory Comtee., 1909-1920; to Colonial Economic Development Comtee., 20; sec. and mem., E. African Currency bd., (resigned secretaryship 1921); crown agt. he cols., Dec., 1920; dir. of colonial scholars, ; rep. of C.O. on exec. coun. of Br. Empire bn., 1921-25; chmn., Palestine currency bd., ; mem., Hong Kong currency comsn., 1931.

LDENHUYS, FRANS EDUARD.—B. 1889; , 1910, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, S.A.; ed in U.S.A. at Univs. of Cornell, Wisconsin and California; B.Sc., Cornell , 1913 and Ph.D., 1917; teacher of nature and agrl. science, Grey Coll. Schl., 1918; "Die Landbou-weekblad," Bloemfontein, 1919; first ch., divn. of agrl. economics marketing, dept. of agr., S.A., 1925; under- or agr., 1926; dir., forestry, 1931.

EN, GEORGE. — B. 1894; ed. Westminster Schl.; on mil. serv., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 2nd lieut., The Buffs; wounded in France; clk., C.O., Oct., 1919 to Feb., 1920; miny. , Sept., 1920 to Dec., 1920; C.O., Dec., June, 1922; apptd. cler. offr., C.O., 21st 1922; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st June, 1928. OFFIN, NEVILL ALFRED MALCOLM.—B. ed. Blundell's; pol. probationer, S. ts., 1908; Feb., 1911; asst. supt. pol., Penang, 1912; asst. supt. pol., Malacca and asst. FFITH, CARL.—Asst. auditor, Gold Coast, 1920.

RAHAM, RT. HON. GEORGE PERRY, P.C. (5), LL.D.—B. 1859; ed. High schls., Iroquois Morrisburg, Ontario; journalist; Morrisburg old, Ottawa Free Press, Brockville Recorder; ed to Ontario legislature for Brockville, 1898, and 1905; provl. sec. of Ontario, 1904; with the govt., 1905; leader of the opposition the Ontario legislature, 1907; sworn of the y coun. for Canada and min. of rlways. and ls, 1907; elected to the H. of C. for Brock-, by acclamation, 1907; defeated at g.e., ; re-elec. for S. Renfrew, bye-elec., 1912; did eek re-election at gen.-elec., 1917; re-el. g.e., , 1921; min. of militia and defence, and of l service in King admstn., Dec., 1921; min. ys. and canals, 1922; senator, Dec., 1926.

Wadham Coll., Oxford; B.A.; priv. sec. to gov., Falkland Is. and clk. of and legis. couns., Apr., 1914; lieut., Falk- Is. volr. force, Aug., 1914; capt., 1917; O.C., ; postal censor, 1914; proper offr. of the n, prize ct., 1914; chief clk., col. sec's office, , 1915; ag. comsnr. of currency, 1918; ag. audr., 1920; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, ; clk., exec. coun., 1921; A.D.C. to ag. gov., and to gov., 1922-23; census offr., 1921; ag. asst. col. sec. for various periods between and 1930; ag. col. sec. for various periods -33; ch. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1930; gov.'s dep. on various occasions, 1930 and 1931; compiler, Sierra Leone, Handbook 1924.

HRYS, H. ARHAM, ARTHUR DOUGLAS, F.R.I.B.A., I., Chartered Archt. and Quantity Surv., n., Town Planning Inst.—B. 1890 ; ed. TITCOMBE, FRED. — B. 1885; European stn. mast., F.M.S. Rlys., Dec., 1910; shorthand writer and typist, Jan., 1912; asst. sec. to gen. man., F.M.S. Rlys., Mar., 1924.

ARKER, CECIL JOSEPH, M.C., M.I.C.E.— 1888 ; asst. engnr., Peru, 1910 ; asst. engnr., V.D., F.M.S., Jan., 1911 ; lieut., R.E. old Co.), 1915-19 ; served in France and Italy ; it. in despa., 1917 ; M.C., 1918 ; war and ory meds. ; ex. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Jan., 0 ; sen. ex. engnr., Apr., 1927 ; state engnr., ak, Jan., 1930.
LAND, JOHN STOCKWIN, M.B.E., B.A., M.I.A.—Draughtsman, P.W.D., tal, Mar., 1909; asst. archt., P.W.D., of S. Africa, Apr., 1912; archt., Sept., ec. for pub. wks., June, 1932.

ARKINSON, A. C. C., M.A. (1910), C.M.G. 31), O.B.E.—B. 1884 ; ed. at Epsom and ford : class. demy, Magdalen. 1903-7 ; 1st cls. da., 1905 ; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1907 ; apptd. after apet. exam., 1st cls. clk., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., miralty, Feb., 1908 ; transfd. as 2nd cls. clk., D., 19th Apr., 1909 ; sec., man. comtee. of perial bureau of entomology ; sec. to comtee. std. to investigate relationship between wild mals and trypanosomiasis, 1913-1914 ; asst. v. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 5th Oct., 1914, to Bonar Law, 27th May, 1915, to Mr. Walter ng, 11th Dec., 1916 ; 1st cls. clk., 8th Dec., 17 ; on military service from 16th May, 1915 to 26th May, 1919 ; prin., 1st Apr., 1920 ; priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 1st Oct., 1920 ; asst. sec., 1st Oct., 1925 ; order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, cls. III, 1929 ; asst. under sec. of state, 15th Aug., 1931.

CCLES, LAUNCELOT WILLIAM GREGORY, —B. 1890; ed. Michaelhouse Sch., Natal and cester Coll., Oxford; asst. survr., N. Rho- a, Oct., 1912; on war serv., 1914-20; asst. r., diagrams, Apr., 1921; ag. ch. survr. from , 1922; dep. dir., surveys and examr., dia- ns and surveys, Oct., 1924; ag. dir., surveys 925, 1928 and 1931; comsnr., lands, mines local govt. and M.L.C., 1933 and 1936.
INTER, K. L.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. a Prot., Apr., 1919.

LOUD, SIR FRANCIS LEWIS CASTLE, K.C.B. (31), C.B. (1918)—B. 1875; ed. Cranleigh Schl. King's Coll., London; barrister-at-law (Lincoln's Inn); ent. bd. of agr., 1894; pvte. sec. to Thomas Elliott (1904-5), Mr. Ailwyn Fellowes (rd Ailwyn) (1905) and Lord Carrington (Marquis of Lincolnshire) (1906-7) as presdts. of bd. agr.; head, land branch, 1907-14; asst. sec., 4-19; permtd. sec., miny. of agr. and fisheries, 1920; chmn., bd. of cust. and excise, 1927; mt. sec., min. of lab., 1930; high comsnr. in Canada for H.M.'s govt. in the U.K., 1934.

OYDELL, HON. THOMAS.—Served as engnr. at E & W. Hawthorn; B. 1887; ent. Peters, nr. Newcastle; warden, county Africa, 1903; worked at D. counties, Apr., 1936. br. wks. and al-JOR-GEN., ANDRIES JACOB rban; stood up, D.S.O.—Joined civ. serv., S. African Labour 1896; served Mpfu (native) cam- rban at the Zoutpansberg and Anglo-Boer War, tested by-elected veldt cornet, S. African Repub. 1895-20, 21 and apt., permanent force (staff), Union labour Party, 1912; promoted major, and serveducus; natl. offr., No. 6 and later as dist. staff d tel. a 7 mil. dist., 1914; brig.-major, 1st line, 1924; ag., German S.W.A. campaign, 1915; nator, S. ut.-col., Mar., 1915; dist. staff offr., BOYEal. dist., with rank of temp. lieut-col., lbert 20 emplt., Aug., 1915; brev. lieut.-col., pt., 1915; brig.-major, 2nd mounted brig., E. 1919; 1, Mar., 1916; gen. staff offr. with rank of 1936. leadqrs., 3rd E. War Med., Victory Med., D.S.O., (1917), French Croix de Guerre; men. in desps., for serv. in G.S.W.A. campaign (1918), medal, Anglo-Boer oorlog.

ILSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR SAMUEL HERBERT, C.M.G. (1929), K.C.B., (1927), K.C.M.G. (1923), B.E. (1921), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 73; entd. Army, R.E., 1893; capt., 1904; major 13; bt. lieut.-col., 1916; bt. col., 1917; served in Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Legion d'Honneur, 1915; Commandeur de la Couronne Belge, 1918; Officier Legion d'Honneur, 1918; ench croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix guerre; sec. to overseas defence comtee., 1911-; Gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff ir., 1st grade, 1915; brig.-gen. gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comtee., 19th Nov., 1918; v., Trinidad, 7th Nov., 1921; gov., Jamaica, Sept., 1924; assumed govt., 29th Sept., 1924; permt. under sec. of state for the cols., 6th Aug., 1925; ret., 1933.

BONEILL, T. H. M., B.Sc., M.I.Mech.E., Whitworth scholar.—Ed. at Shrewsbury and Swindon Tech. Coll.; articed to rly. engineering at Swindon (G.W.R.) works; engaged as junr. asst.-engnr. on construction, Severn tunnel; asst.-engnr. on L.S. and M.S. Rly., U.S.A.; asst. to divisional loco. engnr., C.P.R.; engnr. in charge of G.W.R. West London wks. (loco., carriage and wagon dept.); loco. carriage and wagon supt., Jamaica Govt. rly.; engnr. and asst. gen. man., Cent. American rlys.; loco. and carriage supt., Lagos govt. rly., Mar. 1903; ag. dep. gen. man., 2nd Apr. to 17th June, 1907; and from Sept., 1908, to Feb., 1909; capt., Lagos rly. volunteers.

LOVELACE, LOVELACE HARTE.—B. 1858; clk. of the peace, Trinidad, 1876; clk. of cust., 1877; audit offr., 1878; prin. clk., sup. ct., 1887; also comsnr. of oaths; ag. dep. registr., 1896-7-8, 1900, 1903, 1906 and 1907; dep. registr., 12th Dec., 1910.
BRENNAN, G.—Late 1st W. I. Regiment; assistant inspector, Gold Coast Constabulary, 22nd Nov., 1880; employed on mission to the Prah, June, 1884; acted as inspector-general in 1886.
BRENNAN, G.—Late 1st W. I. Regiment; assistant inspector, Gold Coast Constabulary, 22nd Nov., 1880; employed on mission to the Prah, June, 1884; acted as inspector-general in 1886.

CHADWICK, F. M.—Chief clerk at the royal gaol, Trinidad, Nov. 1, 1859; sub-inspector of police, Trinidad, Jan. 1, 1861; and superintendent of military police; and immigration agent at St. Lucia, June 8, 1864; also inspector of prisons; on the cessation of immigration to the colony was, in July, 1869, appointed chief revenue officer in conjunction with his duties as superintendent of police; and inspector of revenue and police, January 1, 1872; treasurer of Grenada, October, 1872.

GIBBS, J. HATCH.—Barrister-at-law, controller of stamps, Victoria, Dec., 1879.

IBBOTT, JOHN THOMAS.—Sub-controller of customs and sub-bookkeeper, Berbice, 16th March, 1875; acting tide waiter, Georgetown, Demerara, from the 9th July, 1869, to the 1st of Feb., 1860; clerk in charge of the colonial bonded warehouse, Georgetown, 1st Feb., 1860; acting sub-controller of customs and sub-bookkeeper, Berbice, from the 9th Sept., 1869, to the 23rd December, 1870; acting assistant government secretary, and acting receiver-general, Berbice, from the 24th Dec., 1870, to the 23rd March, 1872; appointed on the 16th Feb., 1870, 1st clerk to the customs, Georgetown, Demerara; assumed the duties on the 23rd March, 1872; acted as comptroller of customs in 1872, 1874, and 1876.

INGLOTT, FERDINAND VINCENT, C.M.G. (1868).—Educated in a training college in England at public expense, under the personal direction of the late Sir James P. Kay Shuttleworth, Bart., and of E. C. Tufnell, Esq., inspector of schools, to be a "model teacher" of the new system of primary education; teacher of the new system, and director of the Malta government primary schools reformed according to that system, 1839-48; on the suspension of the educational reform, appointed to assist the secretary and chief clerk in the admiral-superintendent's office, and promoted to other situations in the naval department, 1843-55; assistant superintendent of ports to carry out the provisions of the Merchant Shipping Act, 1854-55-58; comptroller of charitable institutions, and ex officio member of council, Jan., 1859-70; executive commissioner for Malta at the London International Exhibition of 1862, and local commissioner for the Paris Exhibition of 1867; collector of customs, and ex officio member of the executive and legislative councils, Feb., 1870-85; postmaster-general, Jan., 1886.

KENNY, SIR EDWARD, KT. BACHEL. (Created 1870).—Born 1800; sat in legislative council of Nova Scotia for 26 years, during 12 of which he was president of that body; called to the Senate, 1867; receiver-general, July, 1867; president of privy council, Oct. 1869; administrator of the government of Nova Scotia for several months in 1870. Retired from the senate, 1877.

Retired from the senate, 1877.

McCLOSKY, JAMES HUGH, M.R.C.S. and L.M., Edinburgh, L.S.A., London.—Late colonial surgeon, member of legislative council, and J.P. for Labuan; late surgeon, orphan asylum, &c., British Guiana; assistant colonial surgeon, Malacca, 1st August, 1877; colonial surgeon and medical officer, immigration department, &c., province Wellesley, 1879; coroner for the province, 1879-81; is a J.P. for the colony; medical officer, leper asylum, Pulo Jeeyah, Penang, 1884.

McCLOSKY, JAMES HUGH, M.R.C.S. and L.M., Edinburgh, L.S.A., London.—Late colonial surgeon, member of legislative council, and J.P. for Labuan; late surgeon, orphan asylum, &c., British Guiana; assistant colonial surgeon, Malacca, 1st August, 1877; colonial surgeon and medical officer, immigration department, &c., province Wellesley, 1879; coroner for the province, 1879-81; is a J.P. for the colony; medical officer, leper asylum, Pulo Jeeyah, Penang, 1884.

MOIR, R. W. D.—Acting assistant government agent, commissioner of requests, and police magistrate, at Mulaitivu, Ceylon, 1856; commissioner of requests at Calpentyn, 1861; assistant government agent at Mulaitivu, 1861; acting landing surveyor, Colombo, April, 1865; appointment confirmed, September, 1865; acting collector of customs, Galle, 1866; assistant government agent and district judge, &c., Trincomalee, 1870; acting government agent, eastern province, 1878; confirmed, 1879; grain commissioner, 1879; government agent and fiscal, N.W. province, 18th April, 1883.

O'CALLAGHAN, CORNELIUS COLPOYS.—Ensign, 1st W.I. regt., Jan., 1857; lieut., Dec., 1858; captain, Oct., 1870; private secretary to governor C. J. Bayley, Bahamas, 1863-64; A.D.C. to governor Sir Rawson W. Rawson, Bahamas, 1865-66; private secretary and A.D.C. to governor Sir Arthur Edward Kennedy, governor-in-chief West Africa Settlements, June, 1869, to Jan., 1872; Hong Kong, April, 1872, to Mar., 1877; and Queensland, April, 1877.

O'CALLAGHAN, CORNELIUS COLPOYS.—Ensign, 1st W.I. regt., Jan., 1857; lieut., Dec., 1858; captain, Oct., 1870; private secretary to governor C. J. TILTON, JOHN.—Entered the Canadian civil service, 1857; civil service commissioner, 1880; deputy minister of fisheries, 30th June, 1884.
TILTON, JOHN.—Entered the Canadian civil service, 1857; civil service commissioner, 1880; deputy minister of fisheries, 30th June, 1884.
TILTON, JOHN.—Entered the Canadian civil service, 1857; civil service commissioner, 1880; deputy minister of fisheries, 30th June, 1884.

VAN KOUGHNET, LAWRENCE.—4th class clerk, department of Indian affairs, Canada, Feb., 1861; 2nd class, Jan., 1866; 1st class, 1st July, 1873; chief clerk, 1st July, 1870; deputy of superintendent-general of Indian affairs, 1st July, 1880, with rank of deputy minister.
WALLINGTON, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A.—Lt., 3rd bat. Oxfordshire Light Infantry; private secretary to governor, Fiji, 1883; to governor, N.S.W., 1885.
WALLINGTON, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A.—Lt., 3rd bat. Oxfordshire Light Infantry; private secretary to governor, Fiji, 1883; to governor, N.S.W., 1885.

WAYLEN, A. B.—Of the medical and vaccine board, and surgeon to Perth Prison, Western Australia, 1875; served for sixteen years in the convict department, as medical officer of the Guildford convict depot and out-stations; acting as immigration agent and officer in charge of poor-house from August, 1872, until the end of the following year.

REECE, L. RICHARD.—Ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. in 1870; called to bar, Inn. Tem., 1871; J.P., Barbados, 1873; coroner for parishes Christchurch and St. George, 1873; ag. provostmarshal and serjt.-at-arms, 1874; ag. pol. mag. of St. Michael's parish, 1875; ag. judge of asst. ct. of appeal, 1875-7-9; comsnr. of probates, 1877; ag. solr.-gen., 1880; registr. of friendly and building societies, 1880; ag. judge of the asst. ct. of appeal, 1880-82; ag. atty.-gen. and ex-officio mem. exec. and legis. couns.; ch. just. (on two occasions for special purposes) and chancellor of the diocese; sole comsnr. to report on a complaint of the vestry of St. Michael (Barbados) against Bridgetown Water Works Co.; comsnr. to report on working of courts of inferior juris. (Barbados); comsnr. to investigate charges against rioters (Barbados); Grenada, 1883, judge of asst. ct. of appeal, 1883; ag. ch. just., St. Vincent, 1884-5; local comsnr. of incumbered estates ct. (St. Vincent), Oct., 1884; comsnr. to examine and report on the govt. schl., St. Vincent; comsnr. to perform special duty in Grenada at the request of S. of S.; res. mag., Jamaica, 1888.
LYNCH, HON. PATRICK JOSEPH.—Formerly M.L.A., W. Aust.; mem. of senate, Comwth. of Aust.; min. for wks., Comwth. of Aust., 14th Nov., 1916.

BAUER, J. C.—Commenced his professional studies at the Engineering College of Munich in 1854, and received a practical training as machinist; was five years in the public works department in India, engaged on constructive designs and estimates for railways, bridges, steam, hydraulic, and other engineering works, and directing works in progress of construction; passed in Hindustani, Ordoo, and Devanagari; resigned in 1870 to return to Europe; entered the R.E. department, became a prize-holder for architectural designs in public competition, and is a member of the Institution of Civil Engineers of Germany; was selected for the appointment of colonial engineer at the Gambia, and is a justice of the peace.

COFFIN, WILLIAM FOSTER.—Called to the Bar of Lower Canada, 1835; appointed assistant civil secretary, Lower Canada, 1838, and in following year, police magistrate; again appointed assistant civil secretary for police purposes, 1840, and subsequently, in same year, commissioner of police for Lower Canada; appointed, 1842, joint sheriff of district of Montreal, which office he resigned in 1851, in consequence of a sudden reduction of salary by statute; ordnance land agent, 1856; commissioner of ordnance and admiralty lands, 1873; declined the lieutenant-governorship of Manitoba, 1872; holds the rank of lieut.-colonel in the Canadian Militia.

DALY, SIR DOMINICK, KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1856).—Was appointed secretary to the province of Lower Canada in 1827 (having previously held the office of assistant secretary in that province for two years), on the union of the provinces of Upper and Lower Canada in 1840; was appointed secretary of the province of Canada, which office he held until 1848; in 1849 was appointed a commissioner of inquiry into the claims, &c. &c., of the New and Waltham Forests, which he held until the close of the commission in 1850-1; appointed lieut.-governor of Tobago, 1851, transferred to the government of Prince Edward's Island in 1854, which he held until 1859; and appointed governor and commander-in-chief of South Australia, Nov. 1861.

GRATTAN, MAJOR H.—Gazetted ensign 80th regiment, on the 15th June, 1855; promoted lieutenant, 2nd Queen's Royals, in Feb. 1857; served with this regiment in South Africa, also throughout the campaign of 1860 in North China, for which he has a medal with two clasps for Taku Forts and Pekin; exchanged in Feb., 1863, as a captain to the 35th Royal Sussex, and with which regiment he served in India until its return to England in 1868, and for one year was attached to the Punjaub frontier force in command of the detachment then holding Fort Akulghur, at Dera Ismail Khan; promoted to a brevet majority on March 28, 1874, appointed in October, 1874, private secretary and aide-de-camp to Lieutenant-general Sir Arthur Purves Phayre, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., C.B., governor of Mauritius, and in 1878, received the thanks of both the secretaries of state for the colonies and war for services rendered.

HART, FREDERICK RALPH, F.R.G.S., F.R.A.S., &c.—Appointed, on the 1st October, 1873, clerk of the peace for the western district, county of St. Patrick (Cedros, La Brea, and Guapo), and clerk of the petty civil courts comprised in the above-mentioned places; is author of "India Bound to Indo-China," "London Bound," "Scrambles in Venezuela," "Notes on the Suez Canal," taken by him on the spot in February, 1872, and published in 1876, &c.; is a member of the geographical society of Paris. Appointed, on the 1st July, 1874, clerk of the peace for Savanna Grande, in the county of Victoria, also for Oropouche, La Brea, and Guapo (circuit), in the western district of the county of St. Patrick, and clerk of the petty civil courts comprised in the above-mentioned districts; is author of a map of Venezuela on the largest scale yet produced, which was drawn by him after his survey in that country in 1872, and now at the geographical society's rooms, Paris; is a French and Spanish interpreter; appointed clerk of the peace, and clerk of the complaint court for the town of San Fernando, 1st Oct., 1878.

JOSEPH, SIDNEY.—Appointed clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, July, 1843; was private secretary to Rt. Hon. Sir Jas. Stephen for seven years, and subsequently acted in the same capacity to Mr. Merivale for ten years; promoted assistant clerk, November, 1858; first class clerk, 30th September, 1872: Parliamentary clerk of the colonial office.

JOSEPH, SIDNEY.—Appointed clerk in the office of the secretary of state for the colonies, July, 1843; was private secretary to Rt. Hon. Sir Jas. Stephen for seven years, and subsequently acted in the same capacity to Mr. Merivale for ten years; promoted assistant clerk, November, 1858; first class clerk, 30th September, 1872: Parliamentary clerk of the colonial office.

KENNY, KT. BACHEL. (Created 1870), HON. SIR EDWARD KENNY. — Born 1800; sat in legislative council of Nova Scotia for 26 years, during 12 of which he was president of that body; called to the Senate, 1867; receiver-general, July, 1867; president of privy council, Oct. 1869; administrator of the government of Nova Scotia for several months in 1870. Retired from the senate, 1877.

KENNY, KT. BACHEL. (Created 1870), HON. SIR EDWARD KENNY. — Born 1800; sat in legislative council of Nova Scotia for 26 years, during 12 of which he was president of that body; called to the Senate, 1867; receiver-general, July, 1867; president of privy council, Oct. 1869; administrator of the government of Nova Scotia for several months in 1870. Retired from the senate, 1877.

KEENS, JAMES HENRY, C.M.G. (1877). — Formerly president of the privy council; assistant justice of the court of common pleas; member of the legislative council, 1845; and a member of the executive committee, Tobago; was master in chancery from 1857 to 1858; police magistrate from 1858 to 1856; administered government of Tobago in 1856, 1857, 1874, and 1877; was president of the last legislative assembly.

LANGTON, JOHN, M.A.—Sat in Canadian parliament from 1851 to 1855, when appointed auditor of public accounts for province; appointed auditor of the dominion, 30th May, 1868; and subsequently, deputy of the minister of finance, and secretary of the treasury board; declined the finance ministry, 1862; has been vice-chancellor of the University of Toronto.

LAURIER, HON. WILFRID. — Born 20th Nov., 1841; educated at L'Assomption College; took degree of B.C.L. at McGill University; called to the Quebec bar, 1865; edited 'Le Defricheur' newspaper for some years; sat in the Quebec assembly for Drummond and Arthabasha from 1867 to 1874, when he was elected for same constituency to house of commons; now represents Quebec East in that body; sworn of the Canadian privy council and was minister of inland revenue, Oct., 1877, until the resignation of the Mackenzie administration, Oct., 1878.

MCLOSKY, JAMES HUGH, M.R.C.S. and L.M., Edinburgh, L.S.A., London.—Late colonial surgeon, member of legislative council, and J.P. for Labuan; late surgeon, orphan asylum, &c., British Guiana; appointed assistant colonial surgeon, Malacca, 1st August, 1877.

ORD, A. R.—Entered the 54th regiment, Dec., 1851; promoted lieutenant 86th regiment, 1855, and to a company, without purchase, in the 65th regiment, 1862; served during the Indian mutiny of 1857-8-9; was present at the action of Rota-ki-Lerai, at the battle and siege and capture of the town and fortress of Gwalior; commanded two companies 86th regiment at the siege and capture of the fort of Powrie; medal and clasp for Central India; served in the New Zealand war of 1863; medal for New Zealand, also medal for Bhootan; was present and commanded a detachment 65th regiment at the attack near the Williamson's clearing; commanded the Fort Alexander, and repulsed two attacks on that post; retired from the army in 1867; and appointed police magistrate, Malacca, Straits Settlements, 10th Jan., 1867; administered the lieut.-government Malacca, from 3rd July to 12th September, 1868; from 19th Dec. 1868 to 3rd February, 1869; from 25th May, 1872 to 22nd March, 1873; from 29th March to 19th April, 1874.

PRESGRAVE, D. C.—Acting police magistrate, Penang; chief clerk in the treasury, Penang, 1st July, 1849; appointed to the municipal department as secretary, April, 1851, acting magistrate of police and commissioner of the court of requests, Penang, 24th Oct., 1864; appointed deputy coroner, Feb., 1865; twice appointed acting magistrate and commissioner of the court of requests, Province Wellesley, in 1865; and again appointed acting police magistrate and commissioner, court of requests, Penang, 9th May, 1870; and duly admitted as an advocate in the supreme court of the Straits Settlements.

source_text: RUPERT'S LAND, 2nd BISHOP OF. Appointed 1865.—RIGHT REV. ROBERT MACHRAY, D.D.—Late dean and fellow of Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge, and vicar of Madingley; graduated in 1855, when he was 34th wrangler. The diocese of Rupert's Land is 37,000 square miles in extent; and has a population of 200,000 souls, and comprehends the whole of the Hudson's Bay Company's settlements (or Prince Rupert's Land); it was erected into a see in 1849, and endowed by a legacy from the late W. Leith, Esq.

STAWELL, KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1857.)—SIR WILLIAM FOSTER STAWELL.—Chief justice of Victoria, 1857; called to the Irish bar, 1839. ST. BERNARD, J. J.—Is a colonial revenue officer, Grenada; assistant clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1867; colonial revenue officer, 1871; acting chief clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1872; acted as postmaster during 1876; acted as chief clerk in the treasury, 1877; chief clerk, colonial secretary's office, 1878 to April, 1880; colonial revenue officer and registrar of births and deaths, Carriacou. April, 1880.

WILLISFORD, F. C.—Was appointed a writer to the Government of Ceylon, 1862; acting commissioner of requests and police magistrate of Upper and Lower Dumbara, 1862; commissioner of requests at Calpentyn, June, 1864; landing and tide-surveyor at Galle, May, 1856; commissioner of requests, Colombo, September, 1867; police magistrate, Colombo, June, 1868; acting collector of customs, Galle, July, 1871; confirmed in appointment, December, 1871.

WILLISFORD, F. C.—Was appointed a writer to the government of Ceylon, 1862; acting commissioner of requests and police magistrate of Upper and Lower, Doombera, 1862; acting court of requests, Avishawalle, 1863; commissioner of requests at Calpentyn, June, 1864; landing and tide-surveyor at Galle, May, 1865; and acting collector of customs, southern province, July, 1865, acting landing surveyor, Colombo, June, 1866.

WATSON, L. W.—b. 1916; ed. Coopers County Sch., London, E.3.; mil. serv., 1944–47; Sudan and Ken. govt. serv., 1949–50; asst. acctnt., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1951; acctnt., 1956; senr. acctnt., 1960; prin. acctnt., 1962–64.

YAP, Pow Meng.—b. 1921; ed. Victoria Institution, H.K., and Camb. Univ.; M.O., H.K., 1948; psychiatric spec., 1953; lect. in psychiatry and ext. examr. in psychology, Univ. of H.K.; consultant psychiatrist to H.M.'s Forces, H.K.; publ. The Latah Reaction (Jour. Mental Science) 1952; Suicide in H.K. (H.K. Univ. Press) 1958; A Diagnostic and Prognostic Study of the Schizophrenias in Southern Chinese (Proceedings of IInd Intern. Congress of Psychiatry, Zurich, 1958).
YORKE, F. G.—b. 1914 ; ed. St. Mary's Coll., St. L. ; labr. inspr., St. L., 1952 ; prin. clk., admin., 1955 ; supt., prisons, staff offr., spec. reserve, police, 1958.
YORKE, F. G.—b. 1914 ; ed. St. Mary's Coll., St. L. ; labr. inspr., St. L., 1952 ; prin. clk., admin., 1955 ; supt., prisons, staff offr., spec. reserve, police, 1958.
YORKE, F. G.—b. 1914 ; ed. St. Mary's Coll., St. L. ; labr. inspr., St. L., 1952 ; prin. clk., admin., 1955 ; supt., prisons, staff offr., spec. reserve, police, 1958.
CAULFIELD, FRANCIS ST. GEORGE, M.I.C.E.—Educated Trin. Coll., Dub.; superintendent, public works, surveyor and registrar of mines, Perak, Aug., 1871; state engineer and surveyor, Jan., 1884.

BENTLEY, G. K.—b. 1925; ed. Salford Gram. Sch.; mil. serv., 1943–46, R.N.; Inland Rev., 1946; Br. P.O., 1948–56; service and sales supt., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1956; asst. tele. contrl., 1960; senr. asst. tele. contrl., 1963; servs. contrl., 1964.

GUDEMANN, ROBERT.—For some time asst. acct. treas., Jamaica; clerk in audit office, Victoria, Mar., 1853; acct. to treas. and established new system of accts., Jan., 1856; services dispensed with on "Black Wednesday," Jan., 1878; inspector of the banks' clearing house in Feb., and recalled to office in the treas. in June; deputy commssnr. of audit, Mar., 1886; under treasurer, Feb., 1887.

TESSIER, ULRIC JOSEPH, I.L.D. — Justice, Court of Queen's Bench and Appeal Province Quebec, 1873; he was mayor of his native city, Quebec, in 1851; sat in the Canadian Parliament for the county of Portneuf, 1850-53; afterwards in the legislative council, 1859-66; was a member of the provincial cabinet in 1861 and 1862; president of the legislative council from 1859 to 1866, when the confederation was proclaimed; was then appointed one of the senators of the Dominion, which position he held until he was named a judge of the superior court; he has been since 1871 dean of the law faculty of the Laval university.

PINE, SIR BENJAMIN CHILLEY CAMPBELL PINE. KNT. BACHEL. (Creat. 1856), K.C.M.G. (1871).—Educated at Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A. 1834, M.A. 1840; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1841; Queen's advocate at Sierra Leone, 1841; acting governor there, 1848; accompanied Capt. Monypenny, R.N., to the Sherbro river, and destroyed a strong stockaded fort, from which outrages had been committed on British trading vessels. In 1849 again proceeded to Sherbro, with a detachment of troops, and put an end to the civil war which had ravaged the country for years; lieut.-governor of Natal, 1849; 1851 received the thanks of her majesty's government for the measures he took to preserve peace in the colony during the Kaffir war; in 1855, led a force of mounted volunteers against the Amabaca tribe, numbering 2,000 men, and completely enforced their submission; governor and commander-in-chief Gold Coast, 1866; lieut.-governor of St. Kitts, May, 1859; is author of some articles in the Encyclopedia Britannica on the "African Colonies;" acting governor, Antigua, 1866; governor-in-chief, Leeward Islands, Feb., 1869; created K.C.M.G. for his services in connection with the confederation of the Leeward Islands; lieut.-governor of Natal, 1878, until 1875; when he retired on pension.

BOTT, ARTHUR MOORE.—Clerk to attorney-general, British Guiana, 1871; clerk, colonial registry office, July, 1874; 3rd clerk, immigration department, Oct., 1876; stipendiary magistrate's Mar., 1879; compiler of Summary Conviction Ordinances of the Colony, 1837 to 1883; chief police magistrate and sheriff of Demerara; has several times acted as special and auxiliary J.P., ag. asst. pol. mag., Georgetown, 1890, to Jan., 1891, and May, 1893, to Feb., 1891; ag. stip. mag. Essequibo Islands, April, 1891, to 1892.
TCHLEY, C.—Entered the colonial office. g. 1868; served in chief clerk's, accountant's and eral departments; assistant clerk, second class, 5; supt. of the Library, 1st Oct., 1880.

OOTH, ROBT. MALCOLM.—Cadet, Fiji, Nov. 3; attached to col. sec. off., Feb., 1884; ditto, mag. off., Tai Levu, July, 1884; at Navua, v., 1884; at y.-gen.'s off., June, 1885 to Oct. 8; passed cadet, 1887; stip. mag., Tai Levu, 8; ditto. Lomai diti, 1893; ditto, Savu Savu I Bua, 1893; confirmed as stip. mag. and comm. sup. ct., 1894.

DOWNE, ALBERT.—Appointed, after a competitive examination, to be a clerk of the lower (now land) division of the civil service, and assigned to Colonial Office, 4th Sept., 1877, to 20th Oct., 1880; clerk in chief secretary's office, Cyprus, 21st Nov., 1880; returned to duty in Colonial Office, Jan., 1883; appointed to act for three years as assistant to the high commissioner for South Africa, April, 1891; acts also as auditor, Basutoland, from Oct., 1892; member of board of enquiry into postal and telegraph administration, Transvaal, April, 1892; ag. Imperial secy., to Mar., 1895; and (jointly with Capt.kins), Mar. to Aug., 1896; asst. to Imperial v. and accountant, Aug., 1895.

CADDY, DUNCAN JAMES, M.D., F.R.G.S., M.B.—Govt. medl. offr., Carriacon, 1892; ed., Merchant Taylors' and King's Coll, Lond. and Dub. Univ.; member Pathol. Soc. Lond., British Med. Assocn., and other learned societies; has contributed papers on medical subjects to the Lancet, Br. Med. Journal and other journals. CADE. CHARLES SHERWOOD.—Captain in the West Cork Artillery Militia; served through the Zulu War. 1878-9; present at the battle of Ulundi; assistant-inspector, Gold Coast Constabulary, Mar., 1880; inspector, 11th Dec., 1882; adjutant and musketry instructor of the Gold Coast Constabulary, Sept., 1882, to May, 1883; local commandant of police, Cyprus, 29th Sept., 1883; also assistant to commissioner, Nicosia, and governor, central prison, Oct., 1884; has acted several times as commissioner.

LARK, ANDREW INGLIS.—Attorney-general of mania in the Fysh government, 29th Mar., 1892; member of the federal council of Austral., Jan., 1888; and of the federation convention, 1.

APPER, ALFRED HOUSTON. — Ed. Royal col. Armagh, and Trin. Coll., Dub., scholar- in classics; B.A. in 1883; cadet, S. S., Nov., l; J.P. and magistrate, May, 1884; passed l examination in Chinese, and attached to best councillor's office, Penang, 1886; 2nd st. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, 1888; district sr. 1889; ag. sen. dist. off., Butterworth, P. W., , 1889; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., April, 1890; protector of Chinese, Singapore, 1893; ag.. Mag. Penang, Sep. 1893; ag. 2nd asst. col. Sept., 1894; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1894, to fl. 1895; asst. protr. of Chinese, Penang, April, l.

ARLILE, EDWARD. gold medallist in law, Melbourne Univ., 1868. — Clerk, census office, Victoria, 20th May, 1861; registrar general's be. 1st Jan., 1862; crown law offices, 1st Mar., 65; called to the bar, Victoria, 1871; parliament- and professional assistant to crown law officers 73; parliamentary draftsman, July, 1879; clerk- assistant, legislative assembly, April, 1882; re- pointed parliamentary draftsman with higher ans, April, 1889; author of "Notes on Victoria Institution," "Victorian Year Book, 1883-4."

source_text: DOWNE, GEORGE.—Associate to the Judges of the Supreme Court of Tasmania, registrar of supreme court in bankruptcy, curator of intestate estates, and marshal of the vice-admiralty court, 1866; was clerk of the peace, deputy sheriff, registrar of the court of requests and of insolvency, Launceston, 1863; recorder of titles, acting registrar, supreme court, and collector of probate duties, 1875; was private secretary to administrators of the government, June, 1874-75; was acting state secretary to Governor Weld, 1876, 1877-79; was appointed private secretary, 1880; private secretary to governor, Straits, 1881, to Mar., 1884; to the administrator of Tasmania, April, 1884; acting private secretary to Sir G. C. Strahan, governor Tasmania, July, 1886, and to administrator Sir W. L. Dobson, 1886-7.

LARE, HENRY LEWIS.—Ed. Rathmines and Trin. coll., Dub.; B.A. (1879), M.B., and Ch. (1880); junr. resdt. med. offr. pub. hosp. Kingston, Jamaica, Apl., 1881; dist. med. offr. River dist. and hosp., July, 1882; transferred ere dist., 1883; to Chapleton, 1888; to Spanish n., 1892; surg. to St. Catherine dist. prison to Middlesex and Surrey cty. gaol; dist. med. Kingston, June, 1896.

ORD, RICHARD.—Ed. at Collegiate, Shaw St., Liverpool; served for three years with a firm of stockbrokers in that town; emigrated to Australia, 1852, and employed for 2 years in aitor's office in Melbourne; returned to England, 1854, and employed for 3 yrs. by the Liverpool Trust; returned to Australia in 1858, and 3 yrs. in a solicitor's office in Ballarat, commenced business as a sharebroker at Daylesford, subsequently as an accountant, auditor and managing agent at Ballarat; elected auditor to the council and town council there by a large majority, and afterwards chosen as town clerk and treasurer successively; after six years' service, on creation of the Melbourne harbour trust, he was chosen as secy., and received numerous testimonials and valuable testimonials on leaving Ballarat, as delegate on behalf of the trust successfully floated loan of 250,000l. in London in 1883, soon after his return appointed one of the managers of the line, and received testimonials from members of the trust, their officers, and the press, on his transfer; resigned, 1892.

UFFY, THE HON. SIR CHARLES GAVAN, M.G. (1877), (Kt. BACH. 1873).—Was called to Irish bar in 1845, but was early engrossed duties as editor of the "Nation" newspaper, one of the leaders of the national party known Young Ireland. Was a state prisoner along with O'Connell in 1844, and Smith-O'Brien in 1848. Member for New Ross in 1852, defeating Thomas Redington, chief secretary for Ireland. Resigned his seat and emigrated to Australia at the commencement of 1856, and practised for a short time at the bar in Melbourne. Was elected a member of the first parliament of Victoria, and in 1857 became minister of public works in the first responsible government. In 1858 became president of the board of land and works. In 1861 was recalled to the home office. In 1867, after two years' absence in Europe, was re-elected to Parliament for the first time, and shortly afterwards was appointed chairman of a royal commission to devise the best means for effecting a federation of the Australian colonies. In 1871 became prime minister; and in the same year was chairman of an intercolonial conference of Australian cabinet ministers to urge on the colonial office the removal of restrictions on colonial legislation, which change has since been effected. In 1876, after two years' absence in Europe, was again re-elected to Parliament by one of the largest constituencies in the colony without personally visiting the district, and on the assembly being dissolved in May, 1877, was unanimously elected to the office of speaker. After three years' service he resigned, in order to return to Europe. Has since published "Young Ireland—a Fragment of History," "Four Years of Irish History," and "The League of North and South."

AHN, D. M., A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Realschool, Hamburg; served apprenticeship under J. A. Ratzki, architect, Altona; theoretically instructed at the Technological Institution, Hamburg; in charge govt. saw mill, Trinidad, Jan., 1877; foreman, public works, Jan., 1888; ag. st. engineer, N. Div., in 1889, 1890, and 1891; st. engineer, N. Div., 1891.

RIXSON, FRANCIS, late Master, R.N.—Served fifteen years on Australian station, principally in surveying and exploring service; superintendent of pilots, lighthouses, and harbours, N.S. Wales, Jan., 1863; organised N. S. W. Naval Bde., 1863, and still commands it; President, marine board, April, 1872.

UDGE, EDWARD ARTHUR.—Magistrate's clerk, on's Town, May, 1856; clerk in Colonial Office, etown, Aug., 1857; acting C.C. and R.M., aqualand, 1861; C.C. and R.M., Namaqua- , 1864; C.C. and R.M., Queenstown, Dec., 6; inspector-general of chests, &c., 1875; act- C.C. and R.M., Capetown, and R.M., Wynberg. , 1877; C.C. and R.M., Worcester, Nov., 1877; and register of deeds, Kimberley, May, 1882; eld in the following commissions: boundaries ordrecht and settlement of Tambookie loca- , 1870; disturbances in Transkei, 1872; railway rs, Port Elizabeth, 1878; Northern Border dis- ances, 1879; Basutoland and Transkei war nditure, 1881.
GREGOR, JAMES COMYN.—2nd lieut. 7th m. K.R.R., 1881; lieut., 1881; sub.-inspector mtd. police, 1884; ag. asst. comsnr., 1; confirmed, 1893.

LAN, COLONEL ALEXANDER.—Served with field force under the late Brigadier Murray, 1860; employed on the 30-mile radius round Shanghai during the summer of 1863 (medal); with the Anglo-Chinese contingent in Colonel Gordon's Taiping campaigns of 1860-64, being present during the operations before Soochow, and siege and storming of Chang-chow (Chinese medal and Precious Star 2nd class); in 1868-69 stationed in Formosa, and assisted the U.S. consul and Mr. W. A. Pickering, C.M.G., in coming a friendly settlement with the savage tribes; took part in the negotiations at Fort Zelandia and withdrawal of British force (thanked in despatches); selected in 1873 to raise a corps of military police for the Treaty district of Newchwang, northern Manchuria, at that time infested by armed banditti, and subsequently commanded force embodied (thanks of the British and French ministers, and of the Imperial High Commissioner, with the brevet of colonel in the Chinese army, and the cross of the Italian Crown, 4th class); served with the Nile expedition of 1884-45, as staff officer and staff officer, and afterwards as commandant at Dal (medal with clasp and Khedive's star); A.D.C. to General Valentine Baker, B.A. in 1885, and actg. dep. inspr.-general of gendarmerie in 1886-7-8 (thanks of Egyptian Government, and Order of the Osmanieh, 4th class); lt. of local forces, Trinidad and Tobago,

O'RORE, THE HON. SIR GEORGE MATTHEW KNIGHT BACHELOR (1880).—Educated at Trin. College, Dublin, of which he is a graduate, emigrated to Victoria in 1852, and settled in New Zealand in 1854; elected to the House of Representatives in 1861, for the Town of Onehunga, and sat continuously for that until it was merged in the elective district of Manukau, for which he has been since 1882; elected speaker of the House of Representatives, July, 1879; and a dissolution taking place in Aug., was re-elected speaker in Sept. following; on both occasions was elected unanimously; previous to this he had been elected chairman of committees of the House of Representatives, viz., in 1871, 1875, and 1877; has been also speaker of the Auckland provincial council for twelve years, from 1865 to the abolition of the province of Auckland in 1876; entered the Waterhouse ministry in 1872, as secretary of crown lands, and minister for immigration Waterhouse resigning the premiership in 73, he continued in the ministry of Sir Julius Wel until 1874, when he resigned; is a member of the New Zealand bar, and in 1879-80 was chairman of the royal commission to inquire into the conditions of the university of New Zealand, and secondary schools of the colony in their relation to the university; was for the 3rd and 4th time unanimously elected speaker of the House of Representatives. May, 1882, and Aug., 1884, respectively; again in 1887; was elected as first chairman of the council of the Auckland University College founded in 1883, and still holds that office; he is also chairman of the board of governors of the Auckland grammar school.

source_text: LELAND, LIONEL THOMAS HILLIER. — Capt., 4th Batt. S. Staffs. Regt. (late lieut., Worcester Regt.); ed. at St. Columba's Coll. and Dublin Univ.; 2nd lieut., Mid-Ulster Art., 1900; P.S. certif.; apptd. to take draft of Irish Yeomanry to S. Africa; attached to 1st. M.I.; operations, 1901-1902 (Queen's medal with five clasps); comsn., R.G.R., passed A. and B.; M.I. certif.; long course Hythe certif.; special topographical employment; served in Canada 3½ yrs., until withdrawal of Imperial garrison in 1905; transfd. to Worcester regt. and posted to 1st Batt., 1905; passed C. and D.; asst. instr. in topography; resigned, Nov., 1908; apptd. 2nd clk. to gov., Windward Is., Dec., 1908; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir. J. H. Sadler, gov. of Windward Is., Aug., 1909; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Mar., 1912.

BRYDON, LEWIS AUGUSTUS.—Served for sixteen years in H.M. 74th Highland regiment. Was present throughout the Kaffir war at the Cape of Good Hope, during 1851-2-3; and name honourably mentioned in "Division Orders," for conduct in the field; medal; proceeded with his regiment to India, in 1854, and during the mutinies commanded the flank companies of the 74th, attached to the Madras field force, under Major-General Sir G. Whitlock, and was present at the capture of Tharapur; medal. In 1872, he was appointed civil commandant of Trekei and Palma, in the eastern district of the settlement at Lagos, West Africa, and in 1875 was transferred to the Houssa constabulary, on the Gold Coast, as assistant inspector; inspector, 1880; inspector-general, 1882.

BUDGEN, J.—Late captain H.M.'s 95th regiment; served as lieutenant in the 95th regiment at the siege and fall of Sebastopol, Crimea medal and clasp, and Turkish medal; served as lieutenant in the 95th regiment in India, throughout the mutiny of 1857-8, and '59, including the siege and fall of the fortress of Ahwah, as assistant field engineer, Lieut.-Col. Holmes, Bombay infantry, commanding; siege and fall of the fort Kotah, as assistant field engineer, Major-General Roberts, Bombay Army, commanding; served at the siege and capture of the fort Powrie, mentioned in despatches by Sir Robert Napier, K.C.B., for services as assistant field engineer; battle of Beejapoor, under Lieut.-Col. Robertson, 25th Bombay Native Infantry; battle of Kotah-ki-Serai, and after three days the eventual capture of Gwalior, mentioned for distinguished service by Sir Hugh Rose, K.C.B.; the surprise and defeat of the rebels at Koondrie, under Major-General Smith, C.B., 3rd Dragoon Guards, "almost Tantia Tope's last appearance in the field;" medal and clasp for Central India; served as assistant inspector of roads and bridges in Mauritius, 1866-7, under Lieut.-Col. Morrison, Royal Engineers; appointed 3rd clerk civil engineer department, Natal, Feb. 17th, 1875.

CURTIS, OSWALD. — Member of the provincial council of the province of Nelson, New Zealand, from 1858 to 1867; elected superintendent of the province of Nelson in 1867; re-elected in 1869, and again in 1873; member of the House of Representatives for the city of Nelson from 1865 to the present time (1788); postmaster-general, telegraph commissioner, commissioner of customs, commissioner of stamp duties, 1872; fellow and member of the senate of the University of New Zealand, and one of the council of governors of Nelson College.

STOLEY, HERBERT CECIL. — Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; sub-inspr. Cape Police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886.
JAYWARDENE, DON ADRIAN ST. VALENTINE, K.C. Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple—B.1877; dist. judge, Colombo, Ceylon, July, 1922; ag. puisne justice, sup. ct., Mar. to Nov., 1923 and from June, 1924.

GORDON, COLONEL E. C. A., R.E.—Served in the Crimean campaign from March, 1864 (brevet-major, Turkish medal, and order of the Medjidie); appointed H.M.'s commissioner for settlement of claims against the Turkish government on account of the Crimean war, 1856; served under Turkish government as inspector of prisons, and subsequently for eight years as member of their board of works; appointed British member of mixed military commission for the demarcation of the rayon of the Servian fortress of Belgrade, Nov., 1862; commanding royal engineer in Jersey, 1868 to 1871; assistant director of works at the War Office, 1871 to 1876; commanding royal engineer, home district, 1876 to 1879, when he was appointed to the royal engineer command at Bermuda; member of legislation council, Bermuda, April, 1880; acting governor and commander-in-chief from 10 June to 14 Sept., 1880, and again from 1 Aug., 1881.

BOWELL, HON. NEWTON WESLEY, K.C.—B. 1867; ed. London, Ont.; barrister; unsuccessful candidate for H. of C. in East York, 1900; leader, liberal party, Ont. legis., 1911, sitting for North Oxford; re-elected, 1914; pres. of the privy coun., Canada, Oct., 1917; elected to H. of C. for Durham Co., Dec., 1917.

LYON, LOUIS GEORGE MACKENZIE, A.M.Inst. C.E. — B. 1892; ed. Inverness Coll. and Trent Coll., Derbyshire; H. M. Forces France and Belgium; asst. harbr. engnr., Lagos, 1924; do., Takoradi, Gold Coast, 1930; engnr-in-ch., aerodrome constrn., Maiduguri, Nigeria, 1936; senr. asst. engr., harbr. dept., 1936.

MAHONEY, FRANK RODERICK, M.I.R.S.E.—B. 1889; ed. Temple Sch.; elect. foreman, F.M.S. rlys., Sept., 1919; asst. telph. engr., Ipoh, Jan., 1922; dist. telph. engr., grade II, Ipoh, Jan., 1927; dist. signal and telph. engr., Grade I, Seremban, Jan. 1931; do., Gemas, Feb. 1933; ch. engr. way and wks. office, Nov., 1934.

SON, MAJOR FRANCIS WALTER FITTON, (1928), D.S.O. (1917).—B. 1881; ed. coll.; entd. R.A., 1899; served in S. 901-2; seconded to W.A.F.F., 30th July, ist. comsnr., Northern Territories, G. st Jan., 1907; asst. col. sec., G. Coast, r., 1911; provincial comsnr., G. Coast, l., 1915; services lent to W.O., Mar., Apr., 1919; served in France, May, 1915, 1918; ment. in desps. 3 times; O.C. forces, and snr. polit. offr., Togoland, 19; represented govt., in final h nding some and that portion of Togoland ceded e by the Franco-British decln. of 10th 19; dep. ch. comsnr., Ashanti, 1928; ch. N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1930.

COOMBE, K. H. S.—b. 1924; ed. Gram. Sch., Poole, Dorset; Br. P.O., 1940–55; asst. contlr., telecoms., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1955; sen. asst. contlr., 1958–61.

HESSE, P. R.—b. 1925; ed. Dartford Gram. Sch., Woolwich Poly., and Imp. Coll., London Univ.; scientific offr. (soil chemist) E.A.A.F.R.O., 1949; sen. S.O., 1955; W.A. rice research station, S.L., 1958; publ. scientific pp. on chemistry of termite mounds, sulphur and sulphates in Lake Victoria and E.A. soils, etc.

CHERMISIDE, SIR HERBERT CHARLES, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1899), K.C.M.G. (1896), C.B.—B. 1850; Roy. Eng., 1870; during Russo-Turkish war, 1876-78, accompd. Turkish troops as mil. attaché; served in Egyp. camp., 1882; and was attached to Egyp. army, 1883 to 1888; in 1896 became Brit. mil. comsnr. and commander of Brit. troops in Crete, and for services there was made K.C.M.G.; appointed to the Curragh district in 1899, and has since served in S. Afr. in commnd. of 3rd infant. div.; gov. of Queensland, 1901; resigned 1904.

SAINTSBURY, JOHN SEYMOUR.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1906; 1st cls. in Evidence, Procedure and Criminal Law, bar exams., 1904; certif. of honour from council of legal educ. at Bar Final, 1905, and a prize of 50L., from the Inn; passed civ. ser. test exam., Barbados, 1882; supernumerary, col. sec.'s off., 1883; in P.O., 1884; 7th clk., P.O., June, 1886; 4th clk., audit off., Sept., 1890; offr. of cust., Dec., 1890; inland rev. offr., Oct., 1905; ag. pol. mag., dist. "E," July to Nov., 1906.

FOWLE, CAPT. HENRY WALTER HAMILTON.—Ed. at Shrewsbury and Clare Coll., Camb.; late 4th Arg. and Suth. Highrs.; ch. sec. for permits, peace presvn. ord., 1902, and regr. of Asiatics, 1903; Roberts' Horse, S.A. war, 1899-1900; seconded for duty under mil. govr., Pretoria, 1900; mil. comsnr. of pol. and asst. prov. marshal, Heidelberg and dist., 1900-1901; asst. comsnr. of pol., Pretoria, 1901; polit. offr. to mil. comsnr. of pol., Johannesburg; mil. intell. offr., Johannesburg and dist.; asst. to dir., mil. intell., army headqrs., and to prov. marshal, army headqrs., 1901-1902; 2 medals, 6 clasps; served on staff of high comsnr., S. Africa since Nov., 1902; is now asst. imp. sec. to high comsnr.; J.P., Transvaal.

HARD, George Edward.—b. 1893; ed. Melbourne High Sch. and Melbourne Univ. (teacher's certs.); on mil. serv. 1940–45, maj.; mstr. European sch. and supervisor, Banaban Sch. Ocean Is., 1929; dir., educ., G. and E. Is. Col., 1945.

KUPERMAN, Joseph, M.B.E. (1942).—b. 1898; ed. Zicron Jacob Sch. and Ecole Professionne le de L'Alliance Isralite Universelle, Jerusalem; apptd. police dept., O.E.T.A.(S), 1918; asst. inspr., Pal. civ. admin., 1920; dist. offr., 1922; dist. offr., grade H, 1940; asst. dist. comsnr., 1946; mem. of comtees. conn. w. Village Admin. Ordice., and Land Transfers Regs.
ROE, Charles John, I.S.O.—b. 1891; stn. inspr., Kowloon-Canton rlways., 1917; supvrsr. and acctnt. imp. and expt. dept H.K., 1920; senr. clerical and acctng. staff, 1927; gen. sec., P.W.D., 1946.

GIBLIN, HON. W. R.—Attorney-general Tasmania, 5 Feb., 1870, to 4 Nov., 1872, 4 Aug., 1873, to 20 July, 1876, and 9 to 13 Aug., 1877; colonial treasurer, 13 Aug., 1877, to 20 Dec., 1878; treasurer and premier, 3 Oct., 1879, to 1 Dec., 1871; attorney-general and premier, 1 Dec., 1881 to 1884; puisne judge, 1885
KRETSCHMAN, J. M.—Acting chief clerk and draughtsman, surveyor-general's office; second class clerk, treasury, Natal, June, 1881.

DELAPEYRE, EDMOND.—Police and additional district magistrate, Port Louis; was called to the bar at the Middle Temple on 14th May, 1866; 1st Mar., 1872, acting district and stipendiary magistrate, Flacq; Mar., 1873, acting district and stipendiary magistrate, Rivière du Rempart; 4th Aug., 1873, acting district and stipendiary magistrate, Savanne; 19th Nov., 1873, acting additional district magistrate, Port Louis; 1st Jan., 1874, acting district and stipendiary magistrate, Savanne; Oct., 1875, district judge, Seychelles; 18th Oct., 1879, acting chief civil commissioner, Seychelles; 1st Jan., 1880, acting stipendiary magistrate, Flacq; 1st April, 1880, acting junior district magistrate, Pamplemousses; 29th May, 1880, acting district magistrate, Port Louis; 17th Aug., 1880, acting senior district magistrate, Port Louis; 28th Jan., 1881, police and additional district magistrate, Port Louis; 3rd Feb., 1881, visiting magistrate, vagrant dépôt; 17th Sept., 1881, member of the Wood and Forest Land Purchase Commission, and of the commission for drafting civil procedure rules for district courts; police magistrate, Moka, 1882.

YONGE, CAPTAIN H. J., late 61st regt.—Inspector of mines, Du Toits Pan, Bultfontein, Jan., 1874.

ANNESLEY, HON. SYDNEY.—Fourth son of Lord Valentia; entered the 16th lancers in 1848, and served with them four years; accompanied the Oxfordshire Militia, during the Crimean war, to Corfu, and remained with them until 1858, when he was appointed A.D.C. to Sir John Young, the lord high commissioner of the Ionian Islands, and served in that capacity until Sir John returned to England; appointed, 1860; was aide-de-camp for one year to Sir Hercules Robinson, the governor of Hong Kong.

ANNESLEY, HON. SYDNEY.—Fourth son of Lord Valentia; entered the 16th lancers in 1848, and served with them four years; accompanied the Oxfordshire Militia, during the Crimean war, to Corfu, and remained with them until 1858, when he was appointed A.D.C. to Sir John Young, the lord high commissioner of the Ionian Islands, and served in that capacity until Sir John returned to England; appointed, 1860; was aide-de-camp for one year to Sir Hercules Robinson, the governor of Hong Kong.

EVATT, H. A.—Was formerly in the army; served in the 90th light infantry, and Ceylon rifle regiment; whilst still on full pay acted as an assistant in the commissioner of roads department in Ceylon, from the 4th Feb. 1842 to 16th May, 1848, when he was appointed to act as commissioner of roads, and continued in that office until the 2nd Nov. 1849; when he resumed his duties as an assistant in that department. He sold out of the army in 1851, and was appointed assistant civil engineer, and commissioner of roads in April of that year. He acted as head of the department in August, 1854, and resumed his own duties in July, 1856. He acted as surveyor-general, with a seat in the legislative council, from May, 1858, to March 1859; and again from Nov. 1860 to Sept. 1861; appointed acting civil engineer and commissioner of roads in Oct. 1865.
